zones.background_md: [Taryn D'Vat](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taryn_D'Vat), an agent of the [Circle of the Unseen Hand](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:The_Circle_of_the_Unseen_Hand_(Faction)), warns you before entering this ominous lagoon: "Two ancient and powerful beings are planning on striking an alliance to claim [Norrath](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Norrath) as their own. This meeting must not be allowed to take place as it would put our future at their feet. If this alliance is struck and their powers combined, Norrath would reel in its wake." The results of your efforts might be beyond your understanding, but action must nonetheless be taken.
zones.notes_md:
- Has around ~8,000,000 HP on Varsoon.
- Clear the adds he spawns (but not Pkzwk Tzkr).
- There is only one encounter, with no trash to clear, like most T5 instances.
- If you do not kill [Overlord Oxulius](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Overlord_Oxulius) before [Pkzwk Tzkr](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Pkzwk_Tzkr), you will receive messages in your chat box in which Oxulius asks for aid from other powerful [evil eyes](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Evil_Eye), [Borxx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Borxx_(A_Meeting_of_the_Minds)), [Broxx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Broxx_(A_Meeting_of_the_Minds)), [Brixx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Brixx_(A_Meeting_of_the_Minds)), and [Braxx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Braxx_(A_Meeting_of_the_Minds)). Each is worth AA experience independent of the others.
zones.access_md: To enter this zone you need to speak to the [NPC](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taryn_D'Vat) outside the entrance at (-911, -5, 361). Every time you zone out from the instance you must speak with this NPC again.

encounters.strategy_md: - Grants access to the raid instance [The Trembling Lagoon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Trembling_Lagoon)

zones.background_md: One of the [drakota assassins](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Drakota) that slew [Lady Vox](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Vox,_the_White_Dragon) is holed up in this remote part of [Everfrost](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Everfrost). Enter here and bring low the foul beast to claim Vox's [Essence Shard of Heart](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Essence_Shard_of_Heart)!
zones.strategy_md:
This is instance uses the same map as the menagerie.

1. Main Gallery
  - There is a wandering mob of a Skeletal Patrol Leader and 4 Skeletal Warrior (lvl 50^^^ epic(x2)). The pathing will take this mob up to each doorway. They are on a repop timer of about 8 min.
  - The doors to the two side chambers and center chamber are guarded by 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 48^^^ epic(x2)). These guards are also on a repop timer of about 10 min. Becareful with AOE close to the side chamber doors because you can pull another set of guards from inside the side chambers.
1. Right hand chamber
  - Just inside the doorway is guarded by 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 48^^^ epic(x2)). On the bed is a Ar'ticae Cultist guarded by 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 48^^^ epic(x2)). Clear this mob to open the other chamber door.
1. Left hand chamber
  - Just inside the doorway is guarded by 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 50^^^ epic(x2)). Behind the crystal is a Ar'ticae Scryer guarded by 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 50^^^ epic(x2)). Clear this mob to open the center vault chamber door.
1. Center Vault
  - The door to the Vault is guarded by Raegh the Protector (lvl 52^^^ epic(x4)).
  - Inside the vault the walls are flanked by 6 Ice Protector (lvl 51 ^^^).
  - Behind Ar'ticae (who is non-aggro) are an Ar'ticae Summoner and 2 Fettered Guardian and 2 enslaved Thrall (lvl 51^^^ epic(x2)). There is also a lamina Vaner (lvl 51). Once you clear this mob, Ar'ticae will immediately become aggro.
  - Ar'ticae has an uncurable noxious, a fire elemental and cold elemental attack.
zones.walkthrough_md:
1. Enter the eastern doors and kill [an Ar'ticae cultist](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/an_Ar'ticae_cultist).
1. Enter the western doors and kill [an Ar'ticae scryer](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/an_Ar'ticae_scryer).
1. Enter the northern doors and slay [Raegh the Protector](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Raegh_the_Protector). Once he's dead enter the doors behind his spawn.
1. Kill [a Ar'ticae summoner](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_Ar'ticae_summoner) to make Ar'ticae attackable.
1. Slay [Ar'ticae, the Frost Maiden](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ar'ticae,_the_Frost_Maiden) and loot her body for the shard.

zones.background_md: The Ascent is a training ground for those who have sworn their lives to the guardians of the temple and into the service of the Awakened. Within these walls the devoted ones train, hoping to one day have the chance to be honored by an audience with the guardians of the temple. Once one of the devoted ones passes all the trials and learns all of the sacred attacks, they are presented with a symbol of their accomplishment. When one holds all of the symbols that represent the sacred attacks, they are allowed access to the chamber of the guardians. Once there, they can request an audience. - *([Ritnoc Flyfeather](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ritnoc_Flyfeather))*
zones.notes_md:
- This raid is not the same as [Ascent of the Awakened: Audience with the Guardian](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ascent_of_the_Awakened:_Audience_with_the_Guardian) (with [Gorenaire](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Gorenaire) and [Talendor](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Talendor)).
- Use this raid instance to complete [The Riddle of Razorwing](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Riddle_of_Razorwing), part of the [Claymore Timeline](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Claymore_Timeline).

encounters.strategy_md:
The primary danger posed by this Dragon is **Breath of Fire**, which spawns 2 flame blobs for every player or pet within the Area of Effect! This will instantly overwhelm a raid!

Place everyone well out of AE range, pull the Dragon and the tank must get him nose into a crook near an alcove. Now it is a long slow burn. MT's healers must joust in between the **Breath of Flame** attacks to heal, then get OUT of AE range before the 50 seconds expires. As soon as the 2 mobs (for the MT) spawn, ST runs in, grabs them, and drags them well out of range and, assisted by all the melee'rs, kills them. DPS can assist a little, but all the long-range DPS (mages and bowmen) should be on the Dragon. Use all hate reducers to make sure the MT does not lose agro or it is all over!

Take your time! Use your hearts and shards! Play the waiting game and you will win.

zones.background_md: Here at the highest chamber of the Ascent, dwell two leaders of the Awakened, [Gorenaire](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Gorenaire) and [Talendor](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Talendor). You must obtain their marks using the gem that [Lord Nagafen](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lord_Nagafen_(The_Arbiter's_Master)) gave you, should you in earnest desire to enter [Deathtoll](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Deathtoll).
zones.notes_md:
- The person who zones in the raid must have completed the access quest [Audience with the Guardian](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Audience_with_the_Guardian). 
- Click on the statue at (-52, 142, 23) to summon [Gorenaire](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Gorenaire).
- Click on the statue at (-55, 141, -23) to summon [Talendor](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Talendor).

zones.overview_md: You've arrived too late! The orcs of the [Deathfist Empire](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Deathfist_Orcs_(Faction)) have already beaten Kaljanar and take her ring of teleportation to [Deathfist Citadel](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Deathfist_Citadel). Make haste!

zones.background_md: The Bloodskull Orcs have been staging attacks against [Freeport](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_City_of_Freeport) and throughout [The Commonlands](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Commonlands) for long enough. [Overlord Lucan D'Lere](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Overlord_Lucan_D'Lere) wants to strike hard and fast against the orcs and has decided that the best course of action is to assassinate the Bloodskull field commander, [Overseer Maulic](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Overseer_Maulic).
zones.walkthrough_md:
There are five waves of orcs, each wave culminating with a [boss fight](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Bloodskull_Valley:_Maulic's_Stronghold_Named_Monsters) that rewards AA. With each successive wave, the mobs become more challenging. The zone is split into three areas:
- The first area is the courtyard outside [The Battered Wall](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Battered_Wall). Here is where [Legioneer Gorlak](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Legioneer_Gorlak) and then [General Drull](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/General_Drull) will spawn.
- The second area is the field on the other side of The Battered Wall and up to the arena/buildings. Here you will have to fight many trash mobs and two [Bloodskull spotters](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_Bloodskull_spotter) (worth AA) that are found on top of the watchtowers. As long as the spotters are up, two [Bloodskull reinforcements](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_Bloodskull_reinforcement) will run from the narrow path in the east of the zone to the center of the zone. If they get there, four [Bloodskull bouncers](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_Bloodskull_bouncer) will spawn and aggro. Thus, it is crucial to kill the spotters asap to stop this process (although one more bouncer mob usually spawns after each spotter is killed). Once all the spawning mobs are down, and the mobs everywhere except [The Bloodskull Arena](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Bloodskull_Arena) are down, head to the arena for a ring event that begins the next stage.
- The third area includes the arena, the buildings and the tents right before the lake. Once the four [Bloodskull gladiators](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_Bloodskull_gladiator) guarding the arena are killed, mobs spawn at the two buildings and other mobs will spawn, immediately aggroing. Once all these mobs have been dealt with, [a Bloodskull High Command](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_Bloodskull_High_Command) will spawn at the tents, with [Rythar the Conjuror](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Rythar_the_Conjuror) afterward, then [Ternag the Sorcerer](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ternag_the_Sorcerer), and then finally [Overseer Maulic](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Overseer_Maulic).
zones.notes_md:
- Every time you enter you must speak with [Amulius Ramio](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Amulius_Ramio) and go through the dialogue options until you say "Alright, give me the key I'm ready". Don't just spam click through the dialogue since you must end the conversation with the above option. If you end the conversation on any other option then you won't have 'Maulic's Stronghold' appear as an option when clicking on the gate to Bloodskull Valley.
  - You must speak with him again if you die or zone out for any reason.
- It is recommended that the adventurer complete [The Commonlands Timeline](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Commonlands_Timeline), [Bloodskull Valley: The Excavation Site](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Bloodskull_Valley:_The_Excavation_Site) and [Bloodskull Valley: The Training Grounds](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Bloodskull_Valley:_The_Training_Grounds) to better understand the themes surrounding [The Bloodskull Orcs](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:The_Bloodskull_Orcs_(Faction)).

encounters.strategy_md: - Hail Amulius, and continue speaking with him to get the option to enter Bloodskull Valley: Maulic's Stronghold.
- Leader of [The Bloodskull Orcs](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:The_Bloodskull_Orcs_(Faction)).

zones.background_md: You stumbled upon a [peculiar gnome](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Klogg_Bindgear_III) in the ashen crags of [Lavastorm](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lavastorm). He proposed a business deal with you to infiltrate a quarry where supposedly there is a group of [dwarves](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/category:Dwarf) under some time type of mind-control that causes them to mine day-in and day-out. If you are able to free them, the gnome could sneak in and take the mined ore for himself! Any other treasure would be yours for the taking, of course.
zones.walkthrough_md:
1. Progress through the dialogue with [Gustad Aleslammer](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Gustad_Aleslammer) to spawn [K'Dal the Deceiver](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/K'Dal_the_Deceiver).
  - Choose your responses wisely, don't just spam the top choice.
1. Slay K'Dal the Deceiver.
zones.access_md: To gain access to the instance, progress through the dialogue with Klogg Bindgear III just outside the entrance in [Lavastorm](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lavastorm) at (-199.63, -4.44, -359.81).

encounters.strategy_md: - Speak with this NPC to spawn [K'Dal the Deceiver](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/K'Dal_the_Deceiver).
- Talk to him to gain entrance to [Maiden's Gulch](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Maiden's_Gulch)

zones.notes_md: - See [Darathar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Darathar) for strategies on how to defeat him.

encounters.strategy_md:
It is recommended that you pull Darathar into the water, and have casters positioned at maximum range to avoid incurable trauma damage. He will cast a raid-wide stun and heals for 25% of his maximum health three times during the fight, at 75%, 50% and 25% health. This means at 75% health, he will heal to full health. Then at 50% he heals to 75% health, and at 25% he heals back to 50% health. After that point, he will no longer heal himself.

His special attack, [Flame of Ages](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Flame_of_Ages), is an elemental area-of-effect spell which does a high amount of direct damage and has a damage-over-time component. He casts this approximately every 50 seconds. Each member should have elemental cure potions to ensure they can cure themselves in case a healer is unable to cure them immediately.

The self-heals only occur once per instance at each health threshold, not per encounter. For example, if you get Darathar below 50% and then the raid wipes, Darathar will only heal once during subsequent fights. If the raid wipes after taking Darathar to 25% health, he will not heal and stun again in that instance.

See the [Talk:Darathar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Talk:Darathar) page for more strategies and suggestions for defeating Darathar.

zones.background_md: All your efforts in the [Overrealm](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Overrealm) have brought you to the maw of this festering boneyard. [Dragons](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Dragon), [Droag](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Droag), [drakota](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Drakota), nay all of [dragonkind](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Dragonkind) hold these grounds to be sacred, so much that to set just one claw inside is punishable by death. Only the four high priests of [The Awakened](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Awakened_(Faction)) can enter unscathed, [Lord Vyemm](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lord_Vyemm), [Harla Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Harla_Dar), [Sharti of The Flame](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sharti_of_The_Flame), and [Ireth the Cold](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ireth_the_Cold). However, your endeavors in the Overrealm have disrupted the grasp that the Awakened once had upon the land, and thrown their organization into an even deeper state of chaos. [Lord Nagafen](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lord_Nagafen_(The_Arbiter's_Master)) seeks to exploit this opportunity. Fortunately for him, he knows just the right adventurer for the job...
zones.overview_md:
- On normal servers, there is no longer an access quest. However, on TLE servers, at least one member of the raid must have completed the access questline.

When you first zone in, there are droags immediately below the ledge you're standing on, as well as two gazers and a flying serpent which move around the area. The gazers can be lethal if they are not pulled separately and turned away from the raid as quickly as possible. Be careful when range-pulling them, they are social.

The flame barrier has been removed from Deathtoll. However, each member of the raid will need to complete [A Mark of Awakening](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_Mark_of_Awakening) and equip the reward to pass through the location where the flame barrier was (where the basilisk is standing). After you've crossed that point, you can replace it with your regular necklace. You do not need the Mark of Awakening equipped for boss fights, it only needs to be in your character's inventory.

There are traps throughout the zone which spawn [carrion worms](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_carrion_worm). It is recommended the raid avoid central areas on the ground and stay as close to the walls as possible. Everyone should follow the same path through the zone and should not move ahead of the tanks. Although Deathtoll is a persistent raid zone, if you leave and return later, the carrion worm traps will reset and can be triggered again.

There are invisible gazers in the area near Fitzpitzle and the hallways leading to the Amorphous Drake. The gazers frequently cast high-damage AoE DoTs (Eye of Fire or Eye of Frost) which must be cured immediately.

Before you engage [Tarinax the Destroyer](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tarinax_the_Destroyer), you must first defeat [Yitzik the Hurler](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Yitzik_the_Hurler), [Fitzpitzle](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Fitzpitzle_(Deathtoll)) and [Cruor Alluvium](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Cruor_Alluvium). If any of these bosses are still alive when you engage Tarinax, he will immediately kill everyone in the raid. You are not required to kill the Amorphous Drake. This change was made when the Kingdom of Sky expansion was unlocked on the Varsoon TLE server.

encounters.strategy_md:
- Will teleport plater underwater. Without a way to breathe underwater the player will drown.
- Joust his stifle effect or cure

- When HP hits ((55/60))%, raid is stunned and he moves to other side
- Short-range large power drain with crushing damage
- several [book minions](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_book_minion) (70^^^) adds spawn during fight
- He will damage/break an equipment item of his target

Clear all drakes on the lower platform in a (clockwise or counterclockwise), leaving the droags for a second clearing, as these have a shorter respawn time. KILL FAST, aiming for clearing in 10-15 minutes! If you're not averaging 13k DPS or better don't bother do this. Move raid to top of stairs and body pull the drakes, again in a clockwise or counterclockwise manner. All can be pulled without aggro'ing Harla Dar. Once all drakes and droags are clear, pull Harla Dar to the blocked wall behind the area where she stands (the zone out) and be careful of knockback.

Pet pull Vyemm to the hallway all the way back at the bottom of the stairs (a rather long pull). The raid group should wait behind the door at the top of the stairs to avoid the frontal AOE on pull. Healers should wait at the bottom of the stairs. The MT waits in the area at the bottom of the stairs to taunt Vyemm while Alzid is distracted. (The goal is to get Alzid to reset after the summoner has run up the stairs, but keep Vyemm with you.) Turn Vyemm to be perpendicular to the hallway. Once properly turned, send in the forces to rush his side. Everyone needs to fight from his belly. He will move you around during the fight, so stay with him and don't get in front or behind him.

Strat -2

Have the raid sit at the top of the side stairs going to the upper level. Have a monk disband and FD pull him to the encounter room. When he resets he will be single pull able. This eliminates the Heals from the Dracon. have everyone move down into the room and stand on one side of the entrance against the wall leading into the arena. Tank will pull him down the stairs and turn him till his head is facing towards the arena and his back legs and tail are in the room. Everyone will attack him from the sides. Tank healers will need to fight him under his belly dirges and backup healers can joust or rotate in on the tank if they are hit with aoe.

Have a Defiler pull with pet to either the pool to front left or front right of him when Tarinax passes raid who is floating in water in one big clump have monk rescue saving defiler who is ultra self warded. Have monk position him in water so he doesnt frontal cone raid then FD guardian takes over then raid moves in and get under him. Grp cure poison in every grp have 2 guardians rotate Reinforcement/reinforcement/rescue/rescue/reinforcement/reinforcement. If he isnt dead by then then you need more dps. Also heal through Tarinax cuz he will be switching targets a lot. 

Have enchanters under water mezzing adds every 45 seconds if anyone dies more adds spawn so either raid doesnt die or you get a ton of adds. Rez people quickly or you will wipe.

- Knockback
- Low dps
- Spawns Bone Devil (70^^^) at 10% HP

zones.notes_md:
- Completion of the former [access quest](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Access_Quests), [Drayek's Chamber](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Drayek's_Chamber_(Quest)) is no longer necessary.
- For the instance involved in the [Prismatic Weapon Timeline](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Prismatic_Weapon_Timeline), see [Drayek's Chamber: Throne of the Kromise King](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Drayek's_Chamber:_Throne_of_the_Kromise_King).

other_sections.walkththrough:
1. Approach the NPC version of [King Drayek](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/King_Drayek) to start the event.
1. Slay the golems that aggro.
1. Slay the two wolves, [Frostbite](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Frostbite_(Named)) and [Misty](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Misty), that aggro several moments after the golems are killed.
1. Kill [King Drayek](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/King_Drayek)
  - He will spawn two sets of [royal guards](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_Kromise_royal_guard), which seem to make him go immune to damage, then a set of skeletons which don't seem to make him immune. Put your back to a wall to avoid being kicked across the room away from the healer. Not much trouble at level 55 with a mercenary but still a challenge.

encounters.strategy_md:
- This is a ring event. After each wave is cleared, just wait and eventually the next wave spawns.
- When King Drayek reaches 80% health 2xlvl52^^^ and 2xlvl52 ^^ Kromise Royal Guard come to his aid.

zones.zone_name: Drayek's Chamber: Throne of the Kromise King
zones.background_md: The leader of the [Kromise](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:The_Kromise_(Faction)), [King Drayek](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/King_Drayek_(Throne_of_Kromise_King)), has abducted a prismatic egg born of the union of [Lord Nagafen](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lord_Nagafen_(Nagafen's_Lair)) and [Lady Vox](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lady_Vox). Get in here and do what's right!
zones.notes_md:
- Do not kill Drayek too fast, as this will break the encounter. If soloing, mentor down to 50, or 55.
- [The Sage of Ages](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Sage_of_Ages) and consequently [Darathar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Darathar) make an appearance at the end.

zones.background_md: One of the [drakota assassins](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Drakota) that slew [Lady Vox](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Vox,_the_White_Dragon) is holed up in this remote part of the [Feerrott](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Feerrott). Enter here and bring low this foul beast to claim Vox's [Essence Shard of Mind](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Essence_Shard_of_Mind)!
zones.strategy_md:
- During the fight, Thulian's will come to the aid of Dythra, running to the island in waves starting around 60% health.
- Dythra appears to be leashed to the front of the idol.
- There will be serious mana drain from the mobs.
zones.walkthrough_md:
1. Clear the beach/jungle of all adds.
1. Slay [Dythra, the Gloombringer](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Dythra,_the_Gloombringer) and loot her body for the shard.

zones.background_md: [Zalak's](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/King_Zalak_the_Ancient) power reaches far and wide. He is a dark and ancient evil that has grown in power through the ages. Enter here and strike low this abomination.

encounters.strategy_md:
Has four adds to start, and he will periodically call in 2-3 54^^^ adds, after which he will drop a ~2000hp AoE.
Additionally, if the Adds reach the named, they will explode for ~3k Focus damage per add.

zones.zone_name: Firemyst Gully: In Pursuit of the One

metadata.entrance: Speak with [[Relana Everglade]], and then select the instance when clicking on the door to Firemyst Gully. {{loc|-1200|-12|709}}

zones.background_md: An ancient necromancer named [Udalan](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Arch_Lich_Udalan) has returned to the gully and regained much of his former strength. The Arch Lich has already begun summoning another army, one much greater than the last. Once he has amassed a large enough army he will unleash it upon his old and closest foe...the city of [Qeynos](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Qeynos). But the terror and wrath will not stop there, he will raise more dead as he goes, creating an army so vast and powerful that none on [Norrath](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Norrath), save the Old Gods, will be able to contend with him!

encounters.strategy_md:
- Named is grouped with 5 skeletons. Bring some aoe dps and they go down fast (each one has only 30-35k hp).
- Summons [a chaotic elemental](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_chaotic_elemental), 53 ^^^ Heroic adds, every so often that stun you for a period of time. You want to have someone dps these down away from the Tank.

- If you write 'Qeynos' backwards you get 'Sonyeq', or [Sony](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Daybreak_Game_Company,_LLC) [EQ](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/EverQuest)! ''(Source - [Chillispike](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/User:Chillispike))

- Talking to Relana will grant you access to [Shattered Stillness](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Firemyst_Gully:_Shattered_Stillness), an epic instance of [The Firemyst Gully](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Firemyst_Gully).  No quest is received, just the access.

zones.background_md: This is the former hideout of the vampire-hunting faction, the [League of Freethinkers](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_League_of_Freethinkers_(Faction)). Ironically, it has been taken over by powerful [vampires](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Vampire) and [werewolves](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Werewolf) led by none other than [Malkonis D'Morte](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Malkonis_D'Morte_(Freethinker_Hideout)). Under the command of [Mayong Mistmoore](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Mayong_Mistmoore), Malkonis is searching for the [Ankh of Ydal](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ankh_of_Ydal_(Quest_Item)), a legendary rune that permanently scarred the face of Mayong in ages past.
zones.walkthrough_md:
- **Crucial Warning**: To engage the final boss, [Malkonis D'Morte](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Malkonis_D'Morte_(Freethinker_Hideout)), you must talk to four NPCs hidden behind breakable walls. These NPCs must be spoken to in a precise order that is randomized with every zone reset. To break the walls, you must place and ignite a barrel next to the wall. There is a limited number of barrels and they do not respawn. If you ignite the barrel next to the incorrect breakable wall, no NPC will be there and you will not be able to engage the final boss. To know which breakable wall has the correct NPC behind it, you must use [Doodaddle's Soul Sensor](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Doodaddle's_Soul_Sensor) while standing next to it. Doodaddle's Soul Sensor is looted from a maroon-colored skeleton named, 'Doodaddle's Remains', which spawns randomly on the ground in either of the two passageways. While using Doodaddle's Soul Sensor in front of a breakable wall, if you get a notice saying 'The sensor is silent', that is the incorrect wall - do not ignite the barrel. If you get a message saying that you hear something on the other side, that is the correct breakable wall - it is safe to ignite the barrel. You will get a fresh barrel in a correctly chosen room. As a safety precaution, do not ever left-click the barrel. Only ever right-click the barrel and you can safely choose to either move it or ignite it.
  - **Furthermore**, this breakable-wall step must be done before you kill the third boss, [Treyloth D'Kulvith](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Treyloth_D'Kulvith). If you kill Treyloth before talking to the NPCs, Malkonis will laugh and fly away.
- Follow these directions exactly:
  1. Before killing any boss, search the passageways for 'Doodaddle's Remains'.
  1. Once found, use the Soul Sensor next to each breakable wall until you find the correct one and remember it.
  1. In the room with the first boss, [Zylphax the Shredder](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Zylphax_the_Shredder), you are given the first barrel. After killing Zylphax, right-click the barrel and move it the correct breakable wall and ignite it.
  1. Speak to the NPC in the room and click through all their dialogue.
  1. Search for the next correct breakable wall.
  1. When found, return to the previous room, pick up the barrel and move it to the correct breakable wall and ignite it.
  1. Repeat this process until all four walls have been broken in the correct order and all NPCs have been spoken to. Malkonis will no longer fly away after Treyloth has been killed.
  1. Kill [Othysis Muravian](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Othysis_Muravian).
  1. Kill [Treyloth D'Kulvith](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Treyloth_D'Kulvith).
  1. Click the painting at (114,3,-52).
  1. Click the lever behind it.
  1. Kill [Malkonis D'Morte](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Malkonis_D'Morte_(Freethinker_Hideout)).
zones.notes_md:
- Fabled raid instance: min. level 100
- Fabled raid instance (challenge raid): min. level 100

other_sections.strategy walkthrough:
1. When you enter the zone there will be three werewolves directly ahead of you. Have two people click the bone piles on either side of the front room simultaneously to make some of the wolves go away and then pull what is left. If you don't do this you end up with 4 or 5 werewolves at one time.
1. [Zylphax the Shredder](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Zylphax_the_Shredder): Pull to the corner as he has a frontal power drain. When he says "Oops I dropped something" you have about 4 seconds to be as far away as you can to avoid a nasty poison AOE called Stench of Death. This AOE hits very fast. IMPORTANT: leave the barrel alone until you locate Doodaddle's Remains (anywhere in the hallways) and loot the sensor.
1. Go to the south hallway, be prepared for trash to come in groups of 4 or 5 at one time. When you get to the last room stay far left when you enter to avoid aggro.
1. [Othysis Muravian](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Othysis_Muravian): Every 30 seconds or so she will summon a heroic add who will heal her. She will also cast Prone to Corruption on a member of the raid, if they don't die from the spell it will spawn an epic x2 add. Have OT pick these up and kill 
1. Go to north hallway, same type of trash as south. Last named will not start dialogue or become aggro until everyone is in his room.
1. [Treyloth D'Kulvith](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Treyloth_D'Kulvith): If you pile into the close corner to the right as you enter he will not aggro until pulled. He will memwipe at 75%, 50%, and 25%. He casts Reaching Rot every 34 seconds or so which is an AOE that hits anything outside of 10m. He continuously casts Searing Rot which is an AOE that hits anything within 5m. Try to stay between those two to take minimum damage. He also puts an arcane detrimental on the raid which must be cured or your spells reflect.
1. [Malkonis D'Morte](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Malkonis_D'Morte_(Freethinker_Hideout)): To spawn the final name, you must have followed the script for the zone (see above). Fight him next to the wall to avoid the knockup, which will spawn a vampire bat. Throughout the fight, Malkonis calls for help from his ancestors, and one of the six coffins in the room will begin to glow. Attack the glowing coffin (using an arrow or other ranged item) to avoid a vampire add. Beware that the coffin to explode when it is destroyed, doing damage to the group.

encounters.strategy_md: Othysis Muravian's auto attack does crushing and disease damage.  Every 34 seconds will emote, "Through the portal now", and summon a [D'Morte preserver](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_D'Morte_preserver), a heroic mob which will heal Othysis Muravian with Gift of Rejuvenation and Superior Gift of Rejuvenation for 12.3 million hp if it gets close enough.  Every 30 seconds, she emotes, "Time for some soul searching", and does a Disease DD (Prone to Corruption) to a random person in the raid which will spawn an add (Epic x2) if they DON'T die to it.

- Treyloth D'Kulvith will mem wipe at 75%, 50% and, 25% health.
- You need to fight between 10m and 5m of him to avoid both of his AoEs.
- He also places an arcane detrimental on the raid which causes your spells to reflect.

zones.background_md: Only the bravest of souls would dare to venture within here. Deep inside this hidden sanctum lurks the mysterious and malevolent entity known only as the Seeing. A hoard of treasure is guarded by mighty constructs, obedient servants that will let no one escape with their master's many secrets.
zones.walkthrough_md:
- See [here](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Halls_of_the_Seeing/Spoiler) for a full breakdown of the instance.
Go through the instance killing all named mobs. You will come across NPC versions of [Pain](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Pain) and [Suffering](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Suffering). These become aggro after speaking with [Tolapmuj](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tolapmuj). Tolapmuj will spawn after inspecting the armor racks called, 'Weapons of the Fall', strewn about the entire zone, and finding the correct one - it will give a unique dialogue in your chatbox and upon subsequent inspects it will allow you to 'scrutinize' and the 'flip the switch'. After you've taken care of Pain and Suffering, return to Tolapmuj, and he will gift you [Amulet of Desecration](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Amulet_of_Desecration). Go and slay [An Ancient Bloodbeast](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/An_Ancient_Bloodbeast) and you will automatically receive an upgraded [Amulet of Desecration](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Amulet_of_Desecration_(Vortex)).
zones.notes_md: - There is a named called, [O'zkat the Avenger](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/O'zkat_the_Avenger), who spawns after inspecting the correct wall-mounted 'Ward of the Gambler', but there is no monster article about him.

encounters.strategy_md:
- Scrutinizing a random weapons rack will deliver [Tolapmuj](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tolapmuj) with who you must speak with to attack Pain and [Suffering](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Suffering).
- Frontal AE with 100% power drain and mental damage

- Fears
- Heat based power-drain on target
- Frontal AE 100% power-drain and Mental damage

- To spawn Tolapmuj, as well as [Pain](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Pain) and [Suffering](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Suffering), you must find and scrutinize (inspect) a random weapon rack ('Weapons of the Fall') throughout the zone.

zones.walkthrough_md:
1. Kill all three of the [Sha'ir](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Lockjaw's_Lair_Named_Monsters) to breach the destructible wall at (83, -9, -96). Upon the death of the third one, a red-text emote will display on your screen confirming that the wall has been destroyed.
1. Proceed through and slay [Lockjaw the Ancient](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lockjaw_the_Ancient).
zones.notes_md:
- See [the talk page](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Talk:_{{PAGENAME}}) for a more in-depth walkthrough.
- Once upon a time, the three Sha'ir in the zone had to die within about 10 sec of each other, or else they would respawn and the wall blocking off Lockjaw wouldn't break. This is no longer the case as of 2009 (confirmed to still be true as of 9/4/2010), though the Sha'ir will still respawn and red text will still pop up informing you of their reappearance. Just ignore the text and kill them at your own pace.  Once all three have died once, the wall blocking the northernmost hallway will break and you can move on to Lockjaw himself.

encounters.strategy_md: - Memwipes once at about 50% health. Has a dragon-style tail lash if you stand behind him, so don't do that.

metadata.entrance:
Front entrance: {{loc|-34,-10,-1}}
To arrive there you must past through a secret passage that is revealed by clicking on a chest at {{loc|-53, 0, -11}}.

zones.background_md: This is the heart of evil, where [Mayong Mistmoore](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Mayong_Mistmoore_(Instanced)) broods in the dark. Secluded from the rest of the castle, the inner sanctum is where the most powerful vampires dwell. Listen to that voice in your head, and leave now or face his wrath!
zones.overview_md: Sanctum chaperones wander throughout the instance and respawn. They will aggro even if greyed out. Once in their proximity they will run to nearby stoneskin destroyers and activate them. They also have an auto-stun/silence on the first player they hit. The best way to deal with them is to attack them from afar so they won't aggro the gargoyles.

encounters.strategy_md:
To start have your main raid force sneak past Mayong and hug one of the corners behind his coffin. Have the main tank at max distance from raid as Mayong has two PBAOE spells that he casts every so often that you'll want to avoid as one of them (Blanket of Eternal Night) heals Mayong the closer you are to him. Melee DPS should be jousting before either of these two hit so keep an eye on your timers.

Every so often Mayong will spawn two 'a vampiric protector' adds that should be picked up and burned down from the raid force and then refocus attention on Mayong.

Does cast an ability called 'Unholy Strike' that heals him for 11,500,000 damage. Not sure what triggers this or if you can time it to be interrupted but notice he's done it once on the last few encounters.

metadata.idesc: ''Slay the Drakota and claim the soul shard of Lady Vox!''

zones.background_md: One of the [drakota assassins](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Drakota) that slew [Lady Vox](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Vox,_the_White_Dragon) is holed up in this remote part of the [Thundering Steppes](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Thundering_Steppes). Enter here and bring low this foul beast to claim Vox's [Essence Shard of Soul](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Essence_Shard_of_Soul)!
zones.walkthrough_md:
1. Clear the beach of all adds.
1. Slay [Mjolni, the Bringer of Storms](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Mjolni,_the_Bringer_of_Storms) and loot her body for the shard.

encounters.strategy_md:
- Loot her corpse for the [Essence Shard of Soul](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Essence_Shard_of_Soul) needed for [Fire and Ice](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Fire_and_Ice).
- Clear the beach of all other mobs before engaging Mjolni, as all remaining will immediately aggro when Mjolni is pulled.
- This mob is named after [Mjolnir](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mjolnir), the magical hammer of Thor, Norse God of Thunder.

zones.access_md:
To reach this zone, you will need to go through the instance The Oratorium of Thyr in Solusek's Eye. Once inside you must defeat Flame Lord Thyr for the back door leading to Nagafen's Lair to open up.

To take the elevator at (-316, -595, 187) up from level 7 to 6, **RIGHT** click on icon to left of door and click up.

**ATTENTION : You need to target him then Hail him (default Key is "H", or use the slash command /Hail) in order to engage him!** 

During the fight you'll be exiled to a hanging barred cage. Kill the flame spirits that are burning you, and then send your merc (or another player if you're in a group) to kill Flame Lord Thyr (You can use ranged weapons too.
Once defeated, you ll be freed and can enter Solusek's Eye 

Other Details are covered in the article for quest To Speak as a Dragon.

zones.background_md: There has been an influx of hatred into the world. Neighbors are turning hostile to one another, friends no longer care for each other's company, and couples no longer sleep in the same bed. Something is definitely amiss. Adventurers returning from [Nektulos Forest](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Nektulos_Forest) have reported a new, dark energy emanating from near the entrance to [Darklight Wood](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Darklight_Wood). Apparently, as someone approaches it, their thoughts begin to cloud with anger. Could this be source of the rise in hatred? Could the minions of [Innoruuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Innoruuk) at work here?
zones.strategy_md:
Very easy to farm the first four named for plat in 10 or 15 minutes mentored down to level 90 in COE end game gear.

There is no hard and fast rule for this zone, giving the raid a bit of choice in kill order.  The first two named mobs (Demetrius Crane and Dreadlord D'Somni) are considered to be comparatively straightforward kills and therefore good practice for guilds beginning to raid.  However, the difficulty level rises sharply after that point.  

Different raids have their own preference on which one of these two named mobs to kill first.  However, the stategy for each involves clearing the trash mobs near the zone in and all trash leading up to and surrounding them.  This stage is fairly straightforward as long as you remain aware of three things:

- The trash mobs wander a fairly wide area so it is important to choose a fighting position where the raid can avoid adds; the [spite golems](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_spite_golem) move quite quickly, so be aware of their wandering radius.
- In some areas, there are pairs of [animus haunted chests](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/an_animus_haunted_chest) very close to each other, making a potentially tricky pull.  Do not precast anything (heals, reactives etc) before the tank pulls or you will get both chests heading straight towards the culprit!
- Look above to make sure the flying mobs ([scorn fiends](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_scorn_fiend)) do not add.

Once the first two nameds are down, simply rinse and repeat: kill trash on the way to the named mobs and then take down the nameds themselves (see named info for the relevant strategy for each one).
zones.notes_md: - You must be at least level 75 to enter this zone.

other_sections.entrances:
- [Nektulos Forest](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Nektulos_Forest): North of the entrance to [Darklight Wood](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Darklight_Wood) (-1309, 48, -1509)
- [Neriak, City of Hate](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Neriak,_City_of_Hate): By the [Spires of Innoruuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Spires_of_Innoruuk) (-249, 28, 169)

encounters.strategy_md: - The 'Disciples of Innoruuk' function as the priest guild on Neriak.

zones.background_md: The [frogloks](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Froglok) of [Kugup](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Hidden_Village_of_Kugup) have placed their trust in you. [Chief Kaorf](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Chief_Kaorf_(Hidden_VoK)) has informed you that a [great froglok spirit](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Spirit_of_Marr's_Chosen) has been trapped within [the Temple of Cazic-Thule](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/the_Temple_of_Cazic-Thule) by the vile dragon [Venekor](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Venekor_(Spirits_of_the_Lost)). You must figure out how to penetrate this nigh impregnable bastion of fear, liberate the tapped spirit within and bring [Marr's](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Mithaniel_Marr_(God)) justice to the most viscous [lizardmen](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/category:lizardman) and [amygdalans](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Amygdalan) in the service of [Cazic-Thule](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Cazic-Thule).
zones.walkthrough_md: The objective of this raid instance is to slay everything in sight. Start by clearing the upper terrace with [Atrius the Watcher](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Atrius_the_Watcher) atop the central pyramid. Once he has been killed clear the inner temple area. Upon the death of [Qzyk the Conqueror](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Qzyk_the_Conqueror) at the deepest part of the instance, the entire zone will repopulate with new mobs. Re-clear the inner temple area and make your way back to the upper terrace where [Venekor](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Venekor_(Spirits_of_the_Lost)) will be lying in wait. Slay the dragon and bring freedom to the [spirit of Marr's champion](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Spirit_of_Marr's_Chosen)!

zones.zone_name: The Bloodskull Valley: A Noble Confrontation

metadata.azones: [[Qeynos Capitol District|Qeynos]] or [[The City of Freeport|Freeport]]
metadata.entrance:
'''Good-aligned:''' [[Qeynos Capitol District]] at {{loc|567|-10|162}}
'''Evil-aligned:''' [[The City of Freeport]] at {{loc|-122|-38|40}}

zones.background_md: You and your allies must break into [Bloodskull Valley](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Bloodskull_Valley) and disrupt a weapons deal between the [Bloodskull Orcs](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Bloodskull_Orcs_(Group)) and [Chief Surveyor Remolus](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Chief_Surveyor_Remolus). [The City of Freeport](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_City_of_Freeport) stands in peril should you fail!
zones.strategy_md:
Tactics: This zone throws a curve-ball compared to the others. After killing the first couple trash at the first wall you come up to, there is a zonewide announcement that the Spotter calls for reinforcments. This will send a bunch of orc adds, and will not let up until you get into the tower and kill the Spotter.
This isn't as easy as it sounds. Basically, you have to take the adds as you go, pull one Epic encounter at a time, all the while killing the adds and rarely being out of combat. Mana regen and conservation here is a MUST, as well as moving on quickly once the Epic encounter is dead (whether the adds are necessarily all gone or not). I stress Mana managment because not only are you going to be constantly in combat, but the Orc Mages also power drain. They make good charmed pets though.
Kill the last Epic group in front of the tower entrance and then rush the top of the tower and engage the Spotter. He will break aggro if he leaves the tower, so he must be fought there.
Do not AoE at the top as there are Archers there as well. They will hit you through walls, extremely far away as well, and can't be avoided short of killing them off. It can be done, but they have a ton of hitpoints compared to the regular Epic encounters. They also don't aggro properly (they don't move around and no swinging action) and will continue to do the arrow attack at random people while targetting the MT, so it can be hard to see if they've aggro'd and who they are attacking.
Also, fights in the tower can be a little buggy as the mobs will sometimes be on a different floor and not visible, but can still attack/be attacked.

Once the first Spotter is dead, the rest is easy. Head back towards the entrance, and go south along the zone wall (not the short wall) towards the second tower. If you approach while keeping to the edge of the zone, the Spotter won't see you and send adds until you are practically on top of him. Kill him quick and take down those archers as well if you want to stop them. The orc leaders that wander up and down the wall can be ignored as they don't drop anything or set off anything, they just yell orders constantly.
Legionnaire Kruk isn't a real named, and actually quite an easy fight. Fights only a little harder than the previous regular melee Epics in the zone.

Once done with the second Spotter, clear the way towards Commander Gorzok. He will be on the roof of the middle building, and can be pulled with range if you stand on a rock and jump to get line of sight. He's a pretty simple fight, however he has quite a nasty AoE. It is mental based, but even with resists it's quite damaging. Burn him down quick, let the MT and tanks handle adds if they come (watch for Epic runners), and focus on group heals until he's dead.

With the commander dead, Chief Surveyor Remolus is next. DO NOT try and take him first, as he has some massive AE that vaporizes the raid if the Commander isn't killed first.
Remolus comes with a couple guards, like everyone else, but is otherwise a pushover. He has no AE, and is in fact an easier fight than the Commander without his massive AE.

Oh also, if evil and running home, don't forget that most of the trash hasn't been killed.. don't run into them on autorun like I did.

Boss Specifics: Commander Gorzok is a fighter, most likely a Guardian script. He has mostly slashing combat abilities and melee. He also comes with a mental based AE "Rallying Cry" that does quite a bit of damage to the raid.
Chief Surveyor Remolus is a bard, most likely a Troubadour script. He has crushing attacks and a mental based all (?) resistances debuff. His "Singing Strike" also heals him a little.

Reward: 2 Chests, have seen 1-2 armor drops from either. Legionnaire Kruk, the named right before the entrance to the second tower, always drops a wooden chest that always contains a feysteel item (weapon or armor).

zones.background_md: In the lost shissar city of Chelsith rests a vault built to hide one of the greatest creations on Norrath, the Shissar Calendar. This ancient calendar was built to foretell the fate of Norrath. The shissar used the calendar to foretell the death of their empire. Their attempts to combat that fate and save the shissar race failed. However, one of their creations, a great stone machine, did succeed in containing a small portion of the deadly force that destroyed the Shissar Empire. A small portion of that evil force is more than enough to slay an entire kingdom. Fortunately, a device of great destruction has a guardian of great destruction. The creature that dwells here has done so for ages and for ages it has been transforming its environment, covering much of the secrets of the shissar including a portion of the Shissar Calendar.
zones.notes_md: - Formerly, to gain access to this raid, you or someone in your raid had to be on the quest, [Into the Bowels of the Beast](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Into_the_Bowels_of_the_Beast). However, this is no longer necessary.

encounters.strategy_md:
To get to him, use the [Sebilisian Staff of Sounding](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilisian_Staff_of_Sounding) that is dropped by the previous encounter to break the barrier at entrance to the room.

The first AOE is a beast, that has to be jousted. Timing it is a bit hard because varies slightly. Bring a lot trauma cure potions because the healers won't have time to cure trauma from everyone that gets hit (and with people jousting, they might be out of range too).

Pet pull does not work because the mob cannot be attacked until someone enters room (he will auto-aggro as soon as you enter). Use a dedicated puller to pull him out of room to the tank spot (many raids use the corner where you probably killed the group before). Try to keep as many people outside the AOE range on pull as possible and debuff him fast. Be sure to use all debuffs that debuff the melee damage of him; he can hit really hard and nearly all damage is melee. 

When he gets to 35%, some pillars will pop in the room where his original position was. You will get zone-wide messages for that. While these are up he will reflect all damage, so just debuff and heal at that time. The player(s) that have the [Sebilisian Staff of Sounding](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilisian_Staff_of_Sounding) (should be some DPS classes, no healer or other class that is needed to keep tank up during that time) should move into the room. It helps to have multiples of these staffs (they are not no-zone) because it increases your chances of destroying the pillar fast. Keep collecting them for your next tries if you cannot kill him first time.

Start using the staffs on the pillars until the pillars are destroyed, which can take some time. You will get a zone-wide message for that again. When the pillars are destroyed, finish off named and you are done with the zone.

For an easy-to-understand write-up of the basic strategy, see [The Chamber of Destiny](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Chamber_of_Destiny).  The strategy below has additional details and hints that some may find useful.

The mob will most likely require more than one visit to conquer.  The initial visits will be used for farming.  Should you fail at killing the mob you will have to farm once again.

**Required tasks (explained later)**

1) Prevent AoEs

2) CURE poison (Dedicated Noxious Remedy or better) - everyone should bring a lot (100 per) or stay home.

3) Complete your objective in 15 minutes or die from AoE.

4) If attempting kill, then no deaths!!

**Trash mobs**

The trash mobs drop Fishman Skin (fishman suit).  They should be given to the parties that can do HUGE spike AoE damage for farming runs.  The key here is repetitive spike damage.  The suits are used to farm and later kill Leviathan.  Make sure to kill the last trash mob by the entrance or feel the wrath of the tentacles.

Trash mobs repop every 2 days therefore you can return to continue your adventure.

**Farming**

When setting up to farm your main objective is to acquire as many Volatile fluids as possible.  Please don't buy the red vial LORE off the market as their are two different fluids and those from Halls of Fate DO NOT WORK here.

To acquire fluids members must be "eaten" by Leviathan.  The Fishman Skin off of the trash must be worn by a party member to enter and kill mobs within his belly.  The fluids are looted from the mobs.  It is recommended the bare bones minimum fluid count to attempt a kill is 50.  Yes - we know you uber folks don't need that many but then why are you reading this strategy? 

Once you have been in the encounter for 15 minutes OR once your suits are gone you die from AoE.

**Preventing AoEs**

If you do not continue to suit and send people into the stomach the mob will begin to AoE and you will wipe therefore you must keep someone "suited up" in the water waiting to be eaten at all times.  Once the suits are gone take the wipe and come back to fight another day.  NOTE!! AoEs are extra bad because an AoE can ruin armor at a rate of 10-20% per AoE. (hope you have repair bots!!)

**Killing the Leviathan**

Once again Fishman Skin suits and fluids are required.  Suits for this attempt are given to anyone that has huge spell crit as this boosts the damage done to the mob.  Also give suits to 2 high melee dps. Kill the trash and gather the suits; once again last trash should be killed by the entrance or the rear corridor to prevent tentacle damage.  You can get to the rear by staying out of the water and running around the right hand side.  Please note the globe North of the mob.

Suit your team up and send them in like before but alternate melee dps with mages ready to fire off fluid vials as quickly as possible. Suited melees keep the center area clear, mages/SK's burn fluids as quickly as possible. To maximize damage inflicted by fluids, make sure single target mitigation debuffs are kept up on Leviathan continually. Undebuffed fluids will do as little as 150k crit damage, while a fully debuffed dispatched Levi will take 340-520k from fluids, so make them count. Despite common misinformation, Levi does _not_reflect combat arts - only spells - which includes snares and taunts. Single target debuffs (Murderous Rake, Dispatch, Obliterated Psyche, Condemn, etc) should be kept up continuously for this purpose. Avoid "green" encounter debuffs (Claras Cacophany, Zander's Choral Rebuff, Despoiling Mist) as these reflect back on whole group, not just caster. If raid is all on same side, Brigand can swim through levi center to get behind for rear debuffs, then swim back to raid without taking much water damage.

Druids should be casting [Serene Symbol](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Serene_Symbol) and brigands should be using [Cuss](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Cuss) as frequently as possible to dispel Reflective aura. 

Apply "The Greenmist Orb" to the globe at 20% (please read excerpt below on the orb). The orb will disappear from inventory once used.

Continue with suits and fluids until mob is dead.

Success!!!

**Additional info on "The Greenmist Orb"**

There has been a lot of questions about how the orb is used within the zone.

This zone requires "The Greenmist Orb" (the purple one) not to be confused by the "Green Mist Orb" (the green one) for the epic weapon update.

The person with the orb must be at the globe located on the North side **BEFORE the mob hits 20%**.  The globe will become activated and is clickable at the same time as the green message appears on the screen.  Click and activate the globe quickly or the opportunity is lost.

If you clicked on it successfully you will get another green message at later time indicating that the orb has been absorbed.  If you failed the raid party will quickly know because the mob will AoE at 1% and kill all.

The orb does not need to be "dropped" into the globe nor does the item get clicked within the inventory or on the hotbar.

**How the Big Boys Do It!**
1. Get your vials out of the guild bank.
1. Kill adds and give skins to mages for kill shot and warriors/scouts for clearing/farm runs
1. Have raid leader organize the suit line-up, two farmers then two mages, rinse and repeat
1. Farmers job is to clear the inside so mages don't die and to loot fluids to keep the GB stalked up.
1. Mages deliver the fluids to kill levi.
1. MT group stands on entrance side of levi and the raid is opposite side.
1. Stand on coral and turn auto attack on.
1. Use nox pots if you don't receive a cure within 3 seconds
1. At 20% health have someone with the greenmist orb scale outside wall over to the webcam looking thing so they are ready At 15% health, inch out and click the webcam when u get the zonewide message, you should see another zonewide message saying something to the effect of levi is now vulnerable.
1. Keep your farm and kill squads rollin' till he dies.

zones.strategy_md:
- Clear the trash
- Activate the names (two upstairs, one down) by right clicking on them. They need to be killed within 10 seconds of each other. Try to kill the machinist last. They need to be killed at the same time to remove the buffs from the boss.
- Pull the main boss upstairs and leave OT downstairs.  The boss has knockback. OT needs to grab and kill the miners that spawn in three places (counter clockwise around the room) at each of the coal piles. The miners cannot reach the main boss, or the main boss heals.
zones.notes_md:
The person to zone in needs the battery from The Mechnomancer (ringevent in Klak'Anon), its not a real quest though. *Only one person in raid needs this power cell* After obtaining this power cell it can be placed in your bank or vault and you will still have access to the zone.

Inside [Klak'Anon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Klak'Anon) NE passage from dam (Not on map)
First part is a ring event. From time of the first kill you have 10 minutes to finish ring event. Kill roamer, adds pop and attack immediately. All are level 72 epic x4. After adds of first round a second pops just inside the factory gate. Killing this guy again pops adds including a named which auto attacks. Defeating the second round aggros the main Clockwork Menace. Doggy has every trick in the book. Killing him lowers the pedestal for further roaming fun. If you do not get to the mob in  by 10 minutes from the first kill your raid is summoned to him for a nice snack.

metadata.entrance: Speak with [[Walheed Raffini]] in [[The Pillars of Flame]] {{Loc|896|-169|-1579}} to gain the option to select this instance from the mirror at {{loc|884|-177|-1595}}

zones.background_md: Very little is known about the Court of Al'Afaz, save the fact that not many seem to return after they enter. It's said to be a place paused at a point in both the cosmos and time itself.
zones.notes_md:
- Don't clear this instance while too high level (everything grey), or the book you need off [Brius Lakado](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Brius_Lakado) will not drop, preventing you from completing the instance.
- All members of the raid entering this zone must hail [Walheed Raffini](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Walheed_Raffini) each time they enter the zone.
- The evac point is outside the zone. If you or any group in your raid uses evac, they will be translocated to [The Pillars of Flame](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Pillars_of_Flame), outside the zone, and will be locked out for 12 hours. Consider taking the group death and reviving at the zone entrance instead of using evac to avoid this problem.
  - A druid using Verdurous Journey does NOT evac you outside the zone however. (tried on 1/21/2015, ended up at the door-mirror within the zone)
- Low faction will get you attacked quicker. Although all named mobs provide guild experience, faction within the zone is only affected by killing the named mobs located in each of the four towers. Each named tower mob will drop your faction with the Al'Afaz court accordingly. At present, there is no known way to improve your faction with the Court. This is important to keep in mind as you ride any elevator in the zone. When elevators arrive at their destinations, you may be immediately attacked if your faction is low enough.
- The majority of loot drops in this zone are no-trade and class specific.
- Make sure to kill [The Keeper of the City Keys](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Keeper_of_the_City_Keys) just inside the zone-in. If you do not kill him other bosses won't spawn, and you'll have to go all the way back to get him.
- All of the mobs in the zone are based on songs from the album [The Court Of The Crimson King](http://www.elephant-talk.com/wiki/In_The_Court_Of_The_Crimson_King) by King Crimson.

other_sections.simple walkthrough:
- After zoning in, kill some of the gardeners around [The Keeper of the City Keys](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Keeper_of_the_City_Keys) to turn him aggressive. 
- Use the lift at (-260, 80, -69).
- Find [Brius Lakado](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Brius_Lakado) wandering around the garden level. In his loot chest contains a book you will need to turn [The Pattern Juggler](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Pattern_Juggler) aggressive.
- After all bosses have been cleared, the elevator in the north tower will be active and you can take it up to kill [The Black Queen](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Black_Queen_(Court_of_Al'Afaz)).

encounters.strategy_md: - 'The Epitaph of Grandius Lakado' will not drop if Brius Lakado is grey.
- Gives access to the raid instance The Court of Al'Afaz.

zones.zone_name: The Cove of Decay: A Heroic Confrontation

'''Good-aligned:''' [[Qeynos Capitol District]] at {{loc|567|-10|162}}
'''Evil-aligned:''' [[The City of Freeport]] at {{loc|-122|-38|40}}

zones.background_md: A [necromancer](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Hairaza_Sollen) has raised the dead crew of a shipwreck in the [Cove of Decay](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Cove_of_Decay). You must stop her before more necromancers answer her call!
zones.strategy_md:
Zone will split an x2 raid into two separate, parallel zones. Balance your groups before you go in, and be prepared to 1-group the zone. Make it a race, see which group finishes first. 

The zone will be rampant with undead, however they can mostly be ignored by running along the wall. Work your way towards the shoreline, and kill Navigator Nina to make Saltwater Saul attackable. Nina now has a stun effect that lasts 2-3 seconds everytime she calls for adds. This has made her fight a little bit harder, but she doesn't do that much dps, so just handle her like the Prince from the firemyst gulley raid.
Once done head over to Saltwater Saul. He has a bunch of rat friends, but they are mostly AE bait and most folks will be competing to get their AE in to be the one that kills them all. Saul is a pretty easy fight, however he has a "kicks sand in your face" that stuns, as well as another ability to stop magic briefly. They are on seperate timers, he seems to alternate between them.

Once dead, Hairaza will get upset and send her spectral sailors over to slow you down. Kill them off (just a single Epic encounter), and then head to a safe spot near Hairaza and watch the event.

A massive Amygdalen called Kor'allaztaraka the Feared will come from the water and rush the raid. He is actually not that hard (fairly cool theatrics though!). He's pretty much entirely single target damage and heals himself very infrequently.  He also breaks the fight if your party isn't doing enough dps, saying "You don't have the numbers to threaten me, weaklings!"  Burn him down fast and this is not a problem.

Immediately after Kor'allaztaraka dies, Hairaza Sollen will aggro and rush the raid. She has a nasty raidwide AE that comes with an annoucnement (stench rolls off the waves or something), that is a disease attack that she spams constantly, every 1-2 seconds. At lvl 64 con, she was hitting our lvl 60 con swashy (who had only 2k resist) for over 2k damage per hit. She has no adds, very little melee.. her only hard part is the disease. It is only encounter wide too, it only hits the group the MT is in (like in AoA). So move the weaker folks to group 2 and avoid the deaths.
Mostly, the encounter is "hard" because it's two bosses back to back... individually they aren't exactly tough. Mana regen makes it fairly easily.

Boss Specifics: Cthulu is a squidface (no class). He has all single target attacks, slashing melee being the largest portion of his damage. He has a poison attack, and a mental based siphon damage/heal.
Hairaza Sollen is a... well she doesn't really do much. She has that one nasty dot, but otherwise just has regular weak and slow crushing melee. Basically, if you can cure or withstand her dot, and handle the fact that she gives you no downtime after the squidface.. she's a pushover.

Reward: 2 Chests, only seen 1 armor drop per chest, although that doesn't preclude the posssiblity for better. Since nearly all trash can be avoided, not sure if rare harvests can drop. Might be worth trying.

zones.zone_name: The Cove of Decay: Epic Angler

metadata.idesc: ''Get rid of the giant and take his fishes!''

zones.strategy_md:
- Position the raid in the water, but as close to the tents by the beach as possible and kill the 52 ^^ heroic fish in the water (grouped encounter). Immediately after the last fish dies, Rognog and his crabs (heroic-epics) will charge the raid.
- Rognog has an AoE knockback that will send everyone flying if they do not have their back against something. At about 25% he will heal to 100% and become a x4.

other_sections.entrance: Speak with [Googegguta](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Googegguta) just outside the entrance in the [Thundering Steppes](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Thundering_Steppes) at (-93, -3, 1011). Complete the dialogue with him to gain the ability to choose this instance in the 'Select Destination' tab for [Cove of Decay](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Cove_of_Decay). Every time you zone out of the instance you must speak with Googegguta again.

encounters.strategy_md: - Speak with him to obtain access to the raid zone [The Cove of Decay: Epic Angler](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Cove_of_Decay:_Epic_Angler).

zones.background_md: Although a crypt, the [Spirit of Vox](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Spirit_of_Vox) persists, giving hope to [Lord Nagafen](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lord_Nagafen_(Nagafen's_Lair)) that one day his [former love](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lady_Vox) can be successfully resurrected.

other_sections.guide:
- If your goal is to speak with [Lady Vox](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lady_Vox) for the quest, [Fire and Ice](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Fire_and_Ice), then while in the instance, the quest-bearer must:
  1. Examine the three essence shards in the following order: Soul --> Heart --> Mind.
  1. Then, interact with the totem in the middle of the platform. This will cause the quest-bearer to die.
    - If soloing, you must find a way to resurrect yourself as there is no respawn inside of the instance. If you release, you will respawn outside of the instance and lose all of your essence shards. The easiest way is to use [Daybreak Cash](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Daybreak_Cash) to resurrect where you died.
- If your goal is bring low the mighty [Spirit of Vox](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Spirit_of_Vox), then just have just have a hapless volunteer march up to the totem and click. They will die. Three groups of ice shards will spawn and attack, followed by the Spirit of Vox herself.

encounters.strategy_md: - She has a 3k cold AE attack, and has an amazing health regen rate. Try to have the tank turn her away from the raid, her AE can be jousted, but if you're quick on the cures (Elemental) you should be ok.

zones.background_md: A [massive rumbler](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tremblar_the_Behemoth) has been discovered living in a potentially lucrative mine in [Zek, the Orcish Wastes](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Zek,_the_Orcish_Wastes). [The Far Seas Trading Company](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:The_Far_Seas_Trading_Company_(Faction)) is guarding the entrance, preventing anyone from getting in and anything from getting out. Should a group of adventurers feel brave enough to slay the beast, the Far Seas Trading Company would be more than happy to allow passage into the mine as well as first access to any riches within.
zones.walkthrough_md:
1. Hail Shyzin the Coercer near to the zone-in and she will ask you to protect her while she casts a weakening spell on the final boss. This will start a long ring event that begins ~5 minutes after first speaking with Shyzin. 
1. Slay the mobs quickly as they spawn, as the next set of mobs will spawn whether or not of the previous set has been killed. 
1. Wave 1 contains [Greater Symbiant](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Greater_Symbiant) and friends.  Wave 2 contains [Symbiant Protector](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Symbiant_Protector) and friends. 
1. Finally, once [Symbiant Lord](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Symbiant_Lord) (Wave 3) has been killed, Shyzin will thank you and state that the weakening spell is complete. [Tremblar the Behemoth](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tremblar_the_Behemoth) will spawn a few minutes later in the eastern part of the zone at (92, 12, -186).
zones.notes_md:
- To enter this instance you must first speak to Gragak Stinkyfeet in [Zek](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Zek,_the_Orcish_Wastes), standing just outside the door at (76, 22, -424).
- **Important Note**: The [named monsters](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:The_Deserted_Mine_Named_Monsters) will periodically damage your gear. If you are too slow about killing them your gear will eventually break. Make sure to bring backup gear, especially weapons if you're playing a class that relies on weapons to use most of their abilities.

encounters.strategy_md: - Speak to Gragak to gain entrance to [The Deserted Mine](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Deserted_Mine).

metadata.entrance: Click 'A Mysterious Prism' at {{loc|-260,225,-53}}. You must have [[Scale of the Master]] in your inventory, which is looted from the chest of [[Siyamak of the Night]] in the [[The Pedestal of Sky|Pedestal of Sky]].

zones.background_md: The [lord](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Djinn_Master_(Monster)) of [The Shimmering Citadel](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Shimmering_Citadel) is found here. You have helped him capture the [twin dragons of night and day](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:The_Pedestal_of_Sky_Named_Monsters), but in the end he betrayed you. Enter here and exact justice on this denizen of the [Plane of Sky](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Plane_of_Sky)!
zones.strategy_md:
The secret to defeating the DM is the djinn weakness to music. This is hinted at throughout the lore. so before entering you will have wanted to complete the quest [The Handcrafted Music Box](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Handcrafted_Music_Box_(Quest)). Having the reward from this quest will trigger the next part.

Upon entering zone a message will appear telling a hint of what song is needed to defeat the DM. Named on the way to the master will drop the song needed.
zones.notes_md:
- In the past, players had to equip a version of the [Scale of the Master](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Scale_of_the_Master), looted from [Servant of Sky](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Servant_of_Sky) in [The Pedestal of Sky](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Pedestal_of_Sky), to gain access to this instance. However, this is no longer required. The player must simply loot the Scale from the chest of the dragons in Pedestal of Sky and they will be able to click on the 'Mysterious Prism'.
- Higher level players must chrono down to 70 or lower so it can drop the chest.
- You may have to disband mercenaries to enter.

metadata.idesc: ''The Bloom of Growth is protected deep within the Emerald Halls.''

zones.background_md:
Before the [Gods](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Gods) departed [Norrath](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Norrath), the Goddess of Nature, [Tunare](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tunare_(Deity)), in an act of mercy for her [creations](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Dal), hid the *Bloom of Growth*, a link between Norrath and the [Plane of Growth](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Plane_of_Growth), deep within [The Lesser Faydark](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Lesser_Faydark) and commanded the dragon [Wuoshi](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Wuoshi) to guard it until she returned. For centuries, the *Bloom of Growth* has sat the head of [The Loa'Sur](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Loa'Sur), feeding the life-giving powers of the river and transforming the Lesser Faydark into what it is today. Wuoshi has continued to feed off of the Bloom's power, growing ever-stronger. Should you be as bold as to attempt to reach the Bloom, you will first have to defeat it's guardian.

The Emerald Halls became the first ever [Persistent Instance](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Persistent_Instance) with [LU37](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/LU37).
zones.notes_md:
- To progress through the raid, kill all the named mobs in each section and the barrier blocking access to the next section will despawn.
- When having all four archetypes present (fighter, mage, priest, scout), you can skip some of the numerous trash mobs when beating the challenge of the NPC, [Askadian Adakus](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Askadian_Adakus). He will give this challenge to a random archetype on each floor. Not having a player of this archetype present, or failing to do the challenge, will result in the zone being more costly in terms of time.
- Names reward AA and are all soloable with mercenaries.
- [Seed of Growth](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Seed_of_Growth) drops from trash mobs are still useful for making [Scroll of Fire Seeds](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Scroll_of_Fire_Seeds).

encounters.strategy_md: To make this mob active, you must kill the three named bixies, [Trixie](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trixie), [Mixie](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Mixie), and [Dixie](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Dixie) at the same time. If you do not kill them within 10 seconds of each other, they will respawn. Pull all three at once, bring each down to 10% and then AoE. As soon as they die, the collector goes active and immediately aggoes with a few adds. She has a fairly large kickback so fight with your back to the walls. You can also pull them from left to right one by one when you burn one down enough, grab the next one. This helps if you're low on heals.

She has a kickback so plant against the wall, mushroom in the center of the room works best since it helps avoid Wuoshi. Every 10% HP she will pop an x2 add, it's best to burn it down and then continue on the mob so that you don't become overrun by adds. Galiel has mana drain and will occasionally drain mana so having some mana regen may be necessary. Adds have a total of 2,500,000 HP.

- Will summon every brownie trickster and prankster in the zone, Kill them first then Hes just a tank and spank.
- Be sure to also kill the Bixies and the Far Strider Unicorn if its up, they will all assist him in the battle if not killed first.
Spawns with 2 adds, a martial advisor and a magical advisor.

Clear centaurs around this mob or they will come help her. Comes with three [a Hedon bloomseeker](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_Hedon_bloomseeker) which have a total of 2,100,000 HP. She has a very mean focus spell that does a lot of damage. She has AOE and short stiffles as well. Buff up and put some wards and burn down her adds and then just ward/heal and burn Sariah down.

Sarik is an endurance fight. The idea of the fight is to outlast the waves of level 74 ^^^ [fang pack members](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_fang_pack_member) that Sarik will spawn periodically. Use your AOE DPS to take out the wolves while the main tank fights Sarik in a save position. When the raid DPS kills the adds they should focus back on Sarik. Our adds totaled to another 4,000,000 HP alone.

Repeat this process until the boss is defeated.

Along with Chilling Mist, this named casts a noxious-based AoE stifle every 45 seconds.  It has a 30 meter range and is curable.  It also memblurs every 25-35 seconds.

The first thing you must do is kill all the other mobs on the first floor.  If any mobs are still alive (including nameds), they will run in to assist.

Place the Main Tank at the big rock and place all the DPS near mushroom.  Put the offtank between the two, on the side nearest to the named, and healers on the other side.  Should look something like this:

 --------R(MT)--------------------
 ---------------------------------
 (H)--------------(OT)-----------T
 ---------------------------------
 -----------(DPS)-----------------

- R= Rock
- MT= Main Tank
- H= Healers
- DPS= DPS
- OT= Offtank
- T= Tender's spawn point

The MT pulls the named, everyone casts AOEs to kill the adds in the encounter. Try to have 1 debuffer ([Brigand](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Brigand) preferable) on the Tender all the times. One DPS ([Assassin](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Assassin) or such) could also stay.

Every 45-60 seconds, 'a seedling storyteller' will pop. They are epic x2.  The offtank must grab the storytellers as soon as they pop, and the raid should to kill them. Once they are dead, kill the Tender.

Kill priority:
1. Encounter adds
1. Storytellers (a Seedling Storyteller)
1. Tender

Comes with a bunch of [a knotted guardian](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_knotted_guardian) adds. The adds are easy to kill and don't do much dps. If you have a lot of AoE, just focus on the named. Adds had a total of 790,000 HP. Make sure you less Wuoshi pass by so she doesn't aggro your raid if it takes too long to burn Treah down.

- Tunare is the deity of most [Fae](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Fae).
- Tunare appears in-game as an older humanoid female wearing flowing robes and a crown of vines and leaves.

- Clear the entire third level before engaging Wuoshi, otherwise all mobs on the level will aggro.
- All of Wuoshi's special attacks drain power.. (AE)
- Wuoshi spawns a Bloodbeast at 75% and 25% health during the fight. (Almost the same as An Ancient Bloodbeast from Halls of the Seeing)
