metadata.entrance:
'''Good-aligned:''' [[Qeynos Capitol District]] at {{loc|567|-10|162}}
'''Evil-aligned:''' [[The City of Freeport]] at {{loc|-122|-38|40}}

zones.background_md: Sources have confirmed the creeping influence of the 'Heretics', an ancient sect of zealots who seek to usurp the leadership of both [Qeynos](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Qeynos) and [Freeport](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Freeport). Enter here and stop them before it's too late.
zones.strategy_md:
Get ready at the top at zone in (buffs, etc). Everyone go downstairs but stay inside tower, have the tank step outside and pull mobs in until the area around the tower is clear. Avoid using out-of-encounter AEs (blue background, etc) because they can hit mobs wandering around the tower and add.
Once the tower area is clear, start pulling each mausoleum. There is a named in each with a couple guards. These named will memwipe and aggro a certain class frequently, calling out the name of the person they are going to attack. See map for the type of class each sister will aggro on memwiping. Healers include power healing, so enchanters should watch out on two mobs.
Once all 4 sisters are killed, the "shield" around the Mother is gone and she can be attacked. It is best to approach from a nice open place where everyone can see each other (the tombstones can sometimes block casting for healers). She will also call adds during the fight, but there is a trick. Start off by focusing entirely on the Mother, and ignore her guards. Don't AE at all, as she seems to bug sometimes (or maybe it's scripted) to call adds faster when her guards are dead.
She will memwipe and target ANYBODY in the raid, that includes anyone that revived up in the tower. DO NOT REVIVE, stay down and let a rezzer (pref paladin, necro or dirge) to rez you. Otherwise she can run off towards the tower, with the adds staying put, splitting the raid force. Better to stay dead the whole fight than risk wiping.
Also, she can bug out if you wipe too often, and become non attackable. Thankfully this zone does not lock until you kill her (hopefully it stays that way).

Boss Specifics: Mother Znedari is a caster, warlock script. Casts a disease and a poison spell, melee's with crushing damage.

encounters.strategy_md: - If you write 'Qeynos' backwards you get 'Sonyeq', or [Sony](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Daybreak_Game_Company,_LLC) [EQ](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/EverQuest)! ''(Source - [Chillispike](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/User:Chillispike))

metadata.entrance: Click the throne at {{loc|-11,-72,176}} to reveal the path to the instance.

zones.background_md:
Deep in the heart of [Karnor's Castle](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Karnor's_Castle) lies the one of the most bloody and gruesome sites in all of [Kunark](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Kunark_(Continent)). When the castle was still a fortress of the [Sebilisian Empire](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilisian_Empire), iksar generals would imprison enemies of the empire deep within the dungeons. Nobles and other important figures were given the special 'honor' of being invited into the deepest part of the dungeon, where they would be subjected to brutal torture, included being de-skinned while still alive, before finally be given the gift of death and tossed into a maw of whirling metal blades. At some point, when the fortress was under the command of a particularly cruel general, the whirling maw was enlarged so it could be used to chop up the captured dragon and giant elites. Amongst the iksar soldiers, this hall of death was given the moniker "The Execution Throne Room".

Decades after the Iksar Empire collapsed, and the fortress abandoned, [Drolvarg](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Drolvarg) took up residence and quickly took a liking to this part of the castle. To entertain themselves, their commanders would dine and rejoice as their [champions](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_drolvarg_champion) would fight [beasts](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_guardian_wurm) or slaughter captured [sarnak](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Sarnak) and [goblins](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Goblin) in some sort of grotesque blood-sport.

Numerous souls have been lost here. The levels of atrocities within this chamber were so great that the spirits of the executed were cursed and bound to personal artifacts taken from them upon incarceration. To this day, these cursed artifacts scream for vengeance against the races that tortured them. Who will hear them against the incessant howling of the drolvarg, or the constant rain that batters the ramparts of the castle?
zones.strategy_md:
Carefully pull the first two guards into the stairway zone in, they're very social and can add two more guards easily; all four can be killed at the same time with a mid 80's and above raid.

Once inside clear the trash mobs around the room by pulling them one at a time up to the raid near the entry killing the champion last down on the main floor. (Which actually leaves Pawbuster for last.)

Pawbuster uses his weapon to strike the floor during the fight causing aoe stuns. Have MT healers at max range and secondary healers beyond them to cure MT healers so they can in turn cure mt and extra tanks. Have all the tanks fight Pawbuster over the grate and trade aggro as he memwipes during the fight. He also has a knockback. His weapon needs to hit the grate in the floor three or four times during the fight to break the lock. When his health hits 20% and he has struck the floor grate at least three times with his weapon have a range dps throw the switch in the floor next to where he originally stood to open the grate below him causing him to fall through the floor and get killed by the massive fan blades.

During the fight with his memwipe he will want to turn on the highest dps so have all the tanks ring him around the grate and fight for aggro. He must be over the grate when the switch is pulled to drop him through.

Not a hard fight just a little chaotic.
zones.notes_md:
- Try and have at least three tanks for this fight with good taunts and at least 4 healers and range DPS.
- No matter what anyone tells you, don't jump down after Pawbuster to loot his corpse. There's no loot down there waiting for you, only a repair bill.

other_sections.soloing quickguide:
The only boss of this instance is [Pawbuster](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Pawbuster). His health will not drop below 10% and he cannot be one-shot. To kill him:
1. Damage him down to 10%;
1. Bring him over the grate in the middle of the chamber, where the whirling blades are.
1. Allow him to use his scripted jump-smash over this grate three times.
  - If done correctly, the first two times he jump-smashes, a blue status update will appear in your chatbox that says, "The mighty blow dealt by Pawbuster scratches the lock on the grate." Upon the third jump-smash, the message will read, "The grate under Pawbuster makes a horrible cracking sound."
1. Run over to the lever at (-5,-2,-73) and pull it. This will open a hole in the grate.
1. Drag Pawbuster over the hole and he will fall in, ending the encounter and dropping the chest outside the grate.

encounters.strategy_md: Many groups have strategies for fighting this mob. The strategies below have all been proven to work with various group makeups in the raids.

zones.access_md: Go to an island off the coast of [The Pillars of Flame](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Pillars_of_Flame) at location (1708.68, -189.84, -842.59) . Click the magic mirror.  There's an epic dragon level 65 near there [Barakah](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Barakah).

other_sections.note:: Talk to the NPCs for lore and to get an idea of the trading system. If you're at max level right now (level 110 gear), this will not be a challenge, but the mobs are green and give loot.  You can see the original document for strategy if you are doing it on level:  [The Djinn Master's Prism](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Djinn_Master's_Prism)  -- however, it's not exactly the same.  You are given a music box and an object that nullifies elemental detriments (see loot below).  The 'trash mobs' don't seem to drop any musical instruments.
other_sections.loot:
The loot table is probably larger than this, but this is a sampling.

- Crate containing Djinn Master's Head (Guild Hall decoration)
- [Superior Djinn Bracelet of the Sky](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Superior_Djinn_Bracelet_of_the_Sky)  - Mythical, when activated summons a mount!
- Purple adornments such as [Winds of Obol](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Winds_of_Obol)
- [Energized Superior Netherworld Robe](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Energized_Superior_Netherworld_Robe) (traded with NPC for level 95 raid gear)
- [Superior Argent Blade of Speed](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Superior_Argent_Blade_of_Speed) (random drop from named)

Always drops:  [Scepter of Unchained Melodies](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Scepter_of_Unchained_Melodies)  and [an ornate musicbox](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/an_ornate_musicbox)  (not house items)

encounters.strategy_md: - This is the non-attackable NPC in Maj'Dul that spawns the dragon form of Barakah. Use a Gold Dragon scale to trigger the attackable dragon.

metadata.azones: [[Qeynos Capitol District|Qeynos]] or [[The City of Freeport|Freeport]]
metadata.entrance:
'''Good-aligned:''' [[Qeynos Capitol District]] at {{loc|567|-10|162}}
'''Evil-aligned:''' [[The City of Freeport]] at {{loc|-122|-38|40}}

zones.background_md: [Prince Mianja](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Prince_Mianja) is attempting to attack [Qeynos](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Qeynos) with an army of [root horrors](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Root_Horror)! Get in there and put an end to the charade!
zones.strategy_md:
Zone will split an x2 raid into two separate, parallel zones. Balance your groups before you go in, and be prepared to 1-group the zone.  Make it a race, see which group finishes first.

Tactics: Follow the left wall towards the small encampment. Kill the guards around the left opening in the short wall. Pull the Prince and prepare for a nasty fight. Along with calling heroic guards, the Prince will also use a raidwide Stun that lasts a few seconds. It's scripted, so it can't be avoided or resisted (it seems).
As usual, burn down the Prince as quickly as possible while MT holds adds. Let the adds burn down from AE, and any other tanks pull adds to the MT if they are on other people. If possible, turn the Stun game back on the mobs and stun them as much as possible (AE preferably) to give the healers a chance to catch up on heals due to the stunning.

Once Prince is dead, head towards the treants. The main area is filled with heroic trees, only kill as needed as they respawn. Kill the three named treants around the perimeter of the zone. They aren't too tough, and come with two heroic guards. This will spawn Piali the Profane in his little evil house.
Piali also has the Stun the Prince used, but on top of that he has a Knockback on a seperate timer. This is dragon sized flining knockback, and will fling you VERY far. So, the best approach is to have the whole raid rush him and fight inside his house. No one leave their back to the entrance, and fight point blank.
Piali has a strong AoE that hurts quite bad, and a fairly long stun (5 seconds or more if I recall), so everyone be ready for being buffetted quite a bit. He also spawns little saplings, but the tanks and MT should be doing enough AoE's to keep aggro. Healers should be focusing as much on group heals as healing the MT.
If you die in here, considering the amount of group healing required, it is easiest to give the rezzers a break and just revive and run back. There's no memwipe to worry about, and you can come back with full power (although with rez effects).

Boss Specifics: Prince Mianja is a fighter, most likely a Bruiser script. Has all crushing attacks and melee. Other than the Stun he's easy.
Piali the Profane is a treant (no class). Has crushing damage attacks. His AoE is crush damage as well, so having low mitigation will get hurt (mages beware!).

Reward: 2 Chests, one for each boss. Have seen 1-2 armor drops from Piali's chest.
zones.notes_md: - All raid members must be mentored to proper level before entering. Zone doesn't auto-mentor like Bold/Daring Confrontation.

other_sections.credits:
- [http://forums.station.sony.com/eq2/posts/list.m?topic_id=346817](http://forums.station.sony.com/eq2/posts/list.m?topic_id=346817)

The Zone is called "The Firemyst Gully: A Gallant Confrontation"

encounters.strategy_md: - If you write 'Qeynos' backwards you get 'Sonyeq', or [Sony](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Daybreak_Game_Company,_LLC) [EQ](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/EverQuest)! ''(Source - [Chillispike](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/User:Chillispike))

metadata.idesc: ''How ironic it is that to reach eternal life I must cross such a wretched place of death.''

zones.background_md: Within lies [Godking Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Godking_Anuk), protector of the Fountain of Life, which is where the once life-giving waters of the Fyr'Un originate. In life, the Godking used the magic water of the Fyr'Un to bring eternal life to all beings within [Ahket Aken](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Living_Tombs). However, at some point the water became corrupted, granting its imbibers an unnatural life. Now, alongside the rest of his defiled city, the Godking has fallen into undeath. He lives out his immortality staring out from within this temple at the heart of his lost city, watching as the sullied waters of the Fyr'Un carry their curse to stone and creature alike.
zones.walkthrough_md:
1. Upon entering the zone, turn around, open the door to the foyer and enter.
1. Kill [Captain Graul Anashite](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Captain_Graul_Anashite) who will have spawned back down the ramp.
1. Proceed west (left from the entrance), around the outside of the temple and clear the trash mobs through to roughly the middle-left of the room.
1. Kill [Eshara](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Eshara) and [Lladan Promanari](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lladan_Promanari) repeatedly until their final versions spawn. (Note: There are three other Epic x2 named mobs that respawn like [Eshara](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Eshara) and have a final form like [Eshara the Risen](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Eshara_the_Risen). All five will give AA XP as do their revived forms)
1. Kill [Eshara the Risen](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Eshara_the_Risen) and [Lladan Promanari the Revived](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lladan_Promanari_the_Revived).
1. Carefully move the raid to the northwest corner of the room.
1. Kill the the Godking and his power and armor manifestations in the following order:
  1. [Power of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Power_of_Anuk), to stop the Godking's fire AOE from triggering
  1. [Armor of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Armor_of_Anuk), to significantly reduce the Godking's high resistance to spells and melee damage
  1. [Godking Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Godking_Anuk)
zones.notes_md:
- As of [LU48](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/LU48) completion of the access quest, [The Eyes of Anashti Sul](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Eyes_of_Anashti_Sul), is no longer required to enter.
- Touching the water anywhere in the zone will spawn [a minion of the Ewer](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_minion_of_the_Ewer).
- Respawn on the trash mobs is fairly short, about 15 in-game minutes.

other_sections.additional pictures:
Image:Fountain open sezme.jpg|Open doors to the Fountain of Life.
Image:Fountain_entrance_view_from_NW.jpg|The entrance as viewed from the northwest corner.
Image:Fountain_east_wall.jpg|The east wall of the inner temple.
Image:Fountain_godking_perch.jpg|The Godking's perch.

encounters.strategy_md: - Spawns adds throughout the fight.

To defeat the Godking you must also defeat the two mobs that are linked to assist him, [Power of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Power_of_Anuk) and [Armor of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Armor_of_Anuk). While three tanks are optimum for this fight, *RaidWiki* reports that it can be accomplished with two tanks, one high-aggro fighter such as a [Berserker](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Berserker) focusing on the [Power of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Power_of_Anuk) and [Armor of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Armor_of_Anuk), and a main tank focusing on the Godking.

Most raiding guilds suggest pulling the [Power of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Power_of_Anuk) via a ranged weapon first which will bring the Godking and [Armor of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Armor_of_Anuk). Be sure the tank fighting the Godking moves him to a corner of the room away from the other fight. Kill [Power of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Power_of_Anuk) first, which will remove the ability of the Godking to cast [Flame Nova](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Flame_Nova). Kill [Armor of Anuk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Armor_of_Anuk) second which will drastically reduce the Godking's defense. Finish off the Godking third.

metadata.idesc: ''It was made by those who are dead, and the dead keep it.''

zones.background_md: Warring factions of undead minions can be found trying to steal or protect the secrets of the ancient city. Do not travel to this place unprepared; it is rumored that only large groups of adventurers stand a chance against the dangers within.* - (Source: [Dungeons in the Desert](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Dungeons_in_the_Desert))*
zones.notes_md:
- has the exact same layout as the [Living Tombs](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Living_Tombs).
- Nearly all named mobs are spawned when a player approaches their location; they are not already up when the instance is created.
- Many mobs here have an AoE stifle ability, which can prove very annoying. To avoid getting hit by it, kite these mobs.
- There is a unique [Work Bench](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Work_Bench) at (-263, 24, 194).

other_sections.entrance: To enter this instance you must first speak with [Muleek Mahaja](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Muleek_Mahaja) in the [Sinking Sands](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Sinking_Sands) at (-357, -144, -1054). Afterwards, when you use the pole used to access the [Living Tombs](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Living_Tombs) you will have the option to enter this instance. Every time you zone out from the instance you must speak with this NPC again.

encounters.strategy_md: Ahk'Mun Rhoen possess a strong knock back effect and a long duration stun. It is highly recommended that you not fight him on the tower where he spawns. Choose one person to climb the tower and aggro Ahk'Mun Rhoen. Then pull him down and have your tank put their back against something to keep Ahk'Mun Rhoen from wandering around while you fight. It is entirely possible to wipe due too the long duration stun if the knock back is not controlled. Other than that it is a fairly straight forward fight. Keep healers unstunned, keep slow on the epic monster and, and keep tanks back to the wall.

- Speak to this NPC to gain the option to enter [The Gates of Ahket Aken](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Gates_of_Ahket_Aken) when attempting to enter [The Living Tombs](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Living_Tombs).

metadata.<!-- optional, if you can zone in from d: Click on the purple orb on the roof of [[The Halls of Fate (POI)|The Halls of Fate]] at {{loc|-3|-359|29}}

zones.background_md: Deep within the lower levels of [The Halls of Fate](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Halls_of_Fate), [Lord Vyemm](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Lord_Vyemm) and his [host of vile creatures](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:The_Laboratory_of_Lord_Vyemm_Named_Monsters) work to exploit the *wurndrom*, creating increasingly foul amalgamations of [droags](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Droag) for an eventual assault on [Norrath](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Norrath). Together with [The Temple of Scale](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Temple_of_Scale), these hallowed halls hold the most powerful dragonkind of the [Overrealm](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Overrealm).

encounters.strategy_md:
Pet pull Vyemm to the hallway all the way back at the bottom of the stairs (a rather long pull). The raid group should wait behind the door at the top of the stairs to avoid the frontal AOE on pull. Healers should wait at the bottom of the stairs. The MT waits in the area at the bottom of the stairs to taunt Vyemm while Alzid is distracted. (The goal is to get Alzid to reset after the summoner has run up the stairs, but keep Vyemm with you.) Turn Vyemm to be perpendicular to the hallway. Once properly turned, send in the forces to rush his side. Everyone needs to fight from his belly. He will move you around during the fight, so stay with him and don't get in front or behind him.

Strat -2

Have the raid sit at the top of the side stairs going to the upper level. Have a monk disband and FD pull him to the encounter room. When he resets he will be single pull able. This eliminates the Heals from the Dracon. have everyone move down into the room and stand on one side of the entrance against the wall leading into the arena. Tank will pull him down the stairs and turn him till his head is facing towards the arena and his back legs and tail are in the room. Everyone will attack him from the sides. Tank healers will need to fight him under his belly dirges and backup healers can joust or rotate in on the tank if they are hit with aoe.

skipped_pages.reason: disambig page; no zone-specific variant found

zones.background_md: The Lyceum was once a quiet retreat for followers of [Harla Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Harla_Dar) to study and enhance their abilities, but the [Amygdalan](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Amygdalan) have now taken the [island](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Fear_Tainted_Isle_(POI))and remade the Lyceum into a ghastly shadow of its former glory. Those [droag](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Droag) not killed were transformed into horrible abominations, persisting even past death to learn from their new masters.
zones.walkthrough_md:
1. Kill all mobs on the first floor to turn the [Thulian fear-guards](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_Thulian_fear-guard) aggro, and then kill them to spawn [Essence of Fear](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Essence_of_Fear). Upon Essence of Fear's defeat, the second level doors open up.
1. [Gnorbl the Playful](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Gnorbl_the_Playful) and [Gnillaw the Demented](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Gnillaw_the_Demented) are optional bosses.
1. Go to [The Sanctum of Fear](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Sanctum_of_Fear_(POI)) and slay [Vilucidae the Priest of Thule](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Vilucidae_the_Priest_of_Thule). (9, 100, -623)

encounters.strategy_md: - The Essence of Fear will AE fear every 20 seconds. (Or more depending on [Fear's Grasp](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Fear's_Grasp))

- Heals from 1% to 25%
- Adds can be mesmerized (as of Jan 2007)
- Nukes hard on 2%

He comes with two large group of adds, which will taunt and therefore cause members of the raid to target them (instead of the MT or MA).  The first set of adds can be killed fairly quickly with AE, so if you get taunted, this is not too much of a problem.  However, the second group of adds are more problematic; if you kill one of them, Gnorbl receives a powerful heal.  This means that nobody in the raid should AoE, use AoE pets or dumbfire pets; the adds will taunt your pets.  The taunt can be overcome by having everybody create the following macro:

/target gnorbl ( this no longer works you need to just keep clicking him or dont attack when the adds spawn ( they despawn after like 10-20 seconds )  )

Spamming this macro will keep you on the named.  The adds he spawns every 30 seconds (Check Special Attacks for info on them).

As long as you don't kill any of the second group of adds, an average raid can take him down in about a minute.

Clear all drakes on the lower platform in a (clockwise or counterclockwise), leaving the droags for a second clearing, as these have a shorter respawn time. KILL FAST, aiming for clearing in 10-15 minutes! If you're not averaging 13k DPS or better don't bother do this. Move raid to top of stairs and body pull the drakes, again in a clockwise or counterclockwise manner. All can be pulled without aggro'ing Harla Dar. Once all drakes and droags are clear, pull Harla Dar to the blocked wall behind the area where she stands (the zone out) and be careful of knockback.

Here's the easiest way to kill Vilucidae: Pet pull Vilucidae out of his room to a side room.  There is no AOE from Vilucidae so everyone can stand BEHIND the mob and melee.  This helps with dps and with aggro, as he will not be pulled out of taunt range.  He hits hard in the first and final stances, so communicating when you need extra heals will really help.  Chain healing in the final stage is recommended.

The reason for this strategy is because in the pit, he spawns these flowers that will cast REALLY long fears on you. You are also unable to leave/enter the pit once the fight begins because a barrier is placed.

metadata.entrance: Third floor of eastern tower. First go {{loc|-258|146|-54}}, then take the elevators up to the top. Click on the mirror.

zones.background_md: The twin dragons, [Barakah of the Day](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Barakah_of_the_Day) and [Siyamak of the Night](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Siyamak_of_the_Night) that you helped [The Djinn Master](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Djinn_Master_(NPC)) capture are unhappy about their current predicament and are eager for to take out their aggression on any witless adventurers.

zones.strategy_md:
Upon entering the zone, all the named skeleton mobs are non-aggro and unattackable. To begin the storyline, search carefully for [Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov) in the room with the unactivated Doomcoil and select the option to make a deal with her via right click. She will tell you a tale about her "raid force" and discuss the possibility of a group effort between your raid and her force. After speaking to Ludmila, have a priest (note : doesnt require a priest, dirge worked fine) resurrect [Jracol Binari](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Jracol_Binari) (again via right click and not the actual spell). 

Once Jracol Binari is resurrected, have a member of the raid take the [Ancient Sebilisian Shards](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ancient_Sebilisian_Shards) from [Meldrath Kloktik](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Meldrath_Kloktik) via right-click. These shards (which are a single item) are used to activate the Vyx skeletons ([Adkar Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Adkar_Vyx), [Iztapa Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Iztapa_Vyx), [Wymbulu Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Wymbulu_Vyx) and [Zykluk Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Zykluk_Vyx)). Proceed around the rooms of the zone to kill these four skeletons.  The order of their deaths doesn't matter for the storyline, but each is slightly different from the others and a successful kill therefore requires a specific resist setup and strategy (see the appropriate named information page for more details).

Kill the two adds first when fighting the four named listed below.  The adds like to mem-wipe and beat on healers and mage classes.

- [Adkar Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Adkar_Vyx) - Mob stifles raid
  - stifled by having casters stand in one corner and the MT keeps the mob pinned and facing into the corner opposite the casters. 
  - The off tank handles the two adds.
  - The raid targets through the off tank until the adds are dead.
- [Zykluk Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Zykluk_Vyx) - Mob fears raid members.  Tank it with a crusader with the STA tree end ability FTW!
  - feared by having casters stand in one corner and the MT keeps the mob pinned and facing into the corner opposite the casters.
  - The off tank handles the two adds.
  - The raid targets through the off tank until the adds are dead. 
- [Wymbulu Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Wymbulu_Vyx) - Mob has knock back
  - booted by having casters stand in one corner and the MT keeps the mob pinned and facing into the corner opposite the casters.
  - The off tank handles the two adds.
  - The raid targets through the off tank until the adds are dead.
- [Iztapa Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Iztapa_Vyx) - Mob has single target massive DD w/power drain
  - casters stand in one corner and the MT keeps the mob pinned and facing into the corner opposite the casters.
  - The off tank handles the two adds.
  - The raid targets through the off tank until the adds are dead.

She's the harm touch/power drain mob.  This fight is now super easy for a Shadow Knight in reckless stance with an INQ merc.  Just cast unholy blessing and hateful respite on yourself and pull.  If she HTs your merc, beat her down.  If she HTs your SK, hit man seive & manastone and beat her down.  The key is to kill her quickly, with your greatly improved DPS from your reckless stance, before she casts a second HT.  She will usually harm touch somebody right off the bat.  If she kills you, don't wait for a rez from the healer.  Hit the revive button ASAP and run back to the room from the nearby entrance instead.  This will allow the healer to hopefully keep themselves and any other group/raid members alive, instead of wasting precious seconds trying to rez the tank.  An alternate strategy when duoing her is to pull her to the entrance right away.  If somebody dies, they just hit revive, and they're right back into the fight.

Each time one of these skeletons is killed, a random member of the raid will receive a shard in their inventory. These four (tradeable) shards are needed to spawn the next named ([Doomcoil](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Doomcoil)). Once all four skeletons are killed and the shards obtained, trade them all to one person and return to the room with Ludmilla and her raid force. 

Move the whole raid into Doomcoil's room and have the person with the 4 shards right click and give the shards to the Meldrath. The adventurers will slowly leave and then after a short pause to talk, they will spawn Doomcoil and cause barrier wards to go up around the room preventing your raids exit. You get one shot at beating Doomcoil before getting locked out of the room. Unfortunately, if left alive, Doomcoil can add in later fights.

Doom coil has a mem wipe and a raid wiping AE.  If the AE goes off in the middle of the raid force, you will have an uphill battle to win.  Recommend casters position in far corner as you entered the room facing doorway.  MT tanks Doom coil by the doorway.
- *Note:  If you die during the encounter (not wipe), revive yourself.  Doom coil will summon you back to the fight.*

Kill the NPC raid force (an encounter based around [Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov)) and then [Imzok's Revenge](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Imzok's_Revenge). If you fail to kill Doomcoil, killing Meldrath first in the adventurers encounter will prevent him from being summoned. The adventurer's healer (Jracol) will group-heal the entire encounter occasionally. The group's tank ([Blorgok the Brutal](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Blorgok_the_Brutal)) and the froglok mercenaries (which are heroic and should be burned down first) have an ability that will taunt and lock target on for a short time. The scout ([Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov)) tends to memwipe and run around causing havoc by attacking whoever she likes and can charm some players. Lastly their mage [Meldrath Kloktik](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Meldrath_Kloktik) will use a relatively weak AE, and can stifle. 
If you have killed Doomcoil, recommended order of kill is: Frogloks, Blorgok, Jracol, Meldrath, Ludmila. If you have NOT killed Doomcoil then the order should be: Frogloks, Meldrath, Blorgok, Jracol, Ludmila.

The encounter for [Imzok's Revenge](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Imzok's_Revenge) is based upon the Staff of Sounding dropped by the adventurers. The staff must be used to break the barrier leading to him. Later, at exactly 35% HP, he will throw up a stoneskin and enrage. A spell effect which looks like lightning flows into Imzok's Revenge when the stoneskin is active.  The person(s) with a staff of sounding (the item persists and thus you can have multiple people with them) must run into the room where Imzok's Revenge was and use them at the two pillars inside his room in order to break the pillars.  The pillars only have a **chance** of breaking with each use of the staff, and since no DPS can do anything during the stoneskin, the more people with staves using them the quicker the two should break. Once both are broken, rocks will fall on Imzok's Revenge and the stoneskin will be removed.  At this point DPS him until he's dead. He does have a trauma-based AE that should be cured that will be used periodically throughout the fight, and hits the tank a bit harder when the enrage/stoneskin is up.
zones.notes_md:
- If soloing, you must be mentored down to at least to level 90. Otherwise, the [Sebilisian Staff of Sounding](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilisian_Staff_of_Sounding) will not drop and you will not be able to reach the final boss.
- Upon the release of [Rise of Kunark](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Rise_of_Kunark) in 2007, this zone, along with [The Execution Throne Room](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Execution_Throne_Room) and [The Tomb of Thuuga](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Tomb_of_Thuuga), served as entry level raids (Tier 1) for the raid progression timeline of the expansion.

other_sections.lore:
Long ago, the Protector's Realm was constructed as a tomb to honor the greatest champions that perished in the fight to unite the first [iksar tribes](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Iksar_Lore) into the [Empire of Ik](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Empire_of_Ik). It is believed that the original ruler of that empire, [Emperor Venril Sathir](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Venril_Sathir), placed great enchantments upon the crypt in honor of the great champions that rest there. This would surely keep any crypt raider out. In truth, the mighty Venril did not enchant this realm in honor of the iksar heroes, but rather, to use the spirits of the champions to act as guardians of ancient secrets. One of the greatest secrets kept in here is bound to the ultimate guardian, the [Sebilite Protector](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Imzok's_Revenge).

The band of crypt raiders that adventurers encounter within this instance is mentioned in a couple other areas outside of the zone. The gnome bounty hunter, [Colonel Leevan](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Colonel_Leevan), from the quest [For a Few Coins More](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/For_a_Few_Coins_More), mentions that he is on the trail of a serial killer that was once a member of a band of crypt raiders.

encounters.strategy_md:
The encounter will turn aggro as soon as the character with the seal approaches it, meaning it is probably safest to have a character with feign death make the approach.

Massive poison/stifle AoE (Haze of Thule) every 45s (varies slightly) does more damage the closer you are (can be resisted): 

- At 0 to 9 metres, it does 13044-25894 poison damage plus 3069-6093 poison damage every 5 seconds. 
- At 10 to 15 metres, it does 8017-12025 poison damage plus 2290-3436 every 5 seconds.
- At 15 to 75 metres, it does 5154-6299 poison damage and 1546-1890 every 5 seconds.  

The stifle will apply to all characters standing between 9 and 75 metres away.  Because the stifle applies equally at 10 to 15 metres as it does at 15 to 75, there is no advantage to be gained by standing in the mid-range.  [Sanctuary](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sanctuary) can avoid the stifle, as can potions and AoE block.

MT pulls to far left corner (with back to corner to avoid knockback), the rest of the raid stays out until Haze of Thule is cast. Joust all the way out of the room every 45s (before Haze of Thule hits) then back in after.  The adds (which fear and stun) can be mezzed, or burn them down first with OT.  Anti-stifle potions, poison and elemental resists, [Sanctuary](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sanctuary) and AoE block will make the job easier.

If the raid wipes but in the process takes down one or both adds, the adds will take a couple of minutes to respawn, making it possible to run in during this time for a slightly easier fight.  Once the adds respawn, the entire encounter will reset and the raid needs to start the fight again using the seal.

Comes grouped with the following as an adventure party that your raid must beat:
- [Jracol Binari](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Jracol_Binari) - Healer
- [Meldrath Kloktik](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Meldrath_Kloktik) - Mage
- 2 [Froglok Mercenaries](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_froglok_mercenary) - Fighters
- [Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov) - Scout

Make sure all party members have maximized their noxious and arcane resists.

To activate Doomcoil, you need shards from defeating [Adkar Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Adkar_Vyx), [Iztapa Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Iztapa_Vyx), [Wymbulu Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Wymbulu_Vyx) and [Zykluk Vyx](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Zykluk_Vyx). Whoever has these fragments initiates the sequence by giving them to the NPC Meldrath. 

Have the raid force move into the room with the inactive Doomcoil.  The whole raid force (apart from the Fighters) stands in the corner near the door.  Fighters stand on the other side of Doomcoil in the far corner so that Doomcoil is between the MT and the rest of the raid.  

Once the fight starts, a barrier goes up around the room.  Every time a member of the raid dies, Doomcoil heals himself for 138,677 health.  If you revive, you will do so outside the room (and hence be unable to re-enter because of the barrier).  This means you only get one chance to kill Doomcoil before the whole raid gets locked out.  Locked out characters will continue to be hit by the AoE.

Once Doomcoil is activated, the Fighters need to keep Doomcoil as far away from the raid as possible to avoid having the AoEs kill the rest of the raid. Melee DPS should joust the AoEs. After casting Sonic Burst, Doomcoil will teleport himself to the door side of the room. He will be immobile for a couple of seconds, during which he will likely target someone other than the tanks. At that point, back off completely until one of the tanks has recovered aggro and moved Doomcoil back to the far corner. Works well to have the tanks coordinate the use of emergency abilities - e.g. Rescue - so that they are not all spent at once. 

- Choking Mist hits every 60s
- Sonic Burst hits every 55s

Killing Doomcoil is all about keeping your raid alive through his aoes, between them he does not hit hard on tank. 
make sure everyone has good resists for both poison and divine. the aoes will hit you everywhere in room but they hit harder if you are closer so jousting helps if you can manage to joust both aoes that will help a lot, but its also doable without if the melees have good resists.
We usually do him with 2 tanks fighting for aggro, and let everyone target mob direct. Then we tell everyone to stop all dps when he is on somoene other than the tanks, and if you see that he is on you then run over to tanks. 
If people don't die to the aoes then its a rather easy fight, but if peeple keep dieing he will keep healing and you will wipe in the end.

To get to him, use the [Sebilisian Staff of Sounding](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilisian_Staff_of_Sounding) that is dropped by the previous encounter to break the barrier at entrance to the room.

The first AOE is a beast, that has to be jousted. Timing it is a bit hard because varies slightly. Bring a lot trauma cure potions because the healers won't have time to cure trauma from everyone that gets hit (and with people jousting, they might be out of range too).

Pet pull does not work because the mob cannot be attacked until someone enters room (he will auto-aggro as soon as you enter). Use a dedicated puller to pull him out of room to the tank spot (many raids use the corner where you probably killed the group before). Try to keep as many people outside the AOE range on pull as possible and debuff him fast. Be sure to use all debuffs that debuff the melee damage of him; he can hit really hard and nearly all damage is melee. 

When he gets to 35%, some pillars will pop in the room where his original position was. You will get zone-wide messages for that. While these are up he will reflect all damage, so just debuff and heal at that time. The player(s) that have the [Sebilisian Staff of Sounding](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilisian_Staff_of_Sounding) (should be some DPS classes, no healer or other class that is needed to keep tank up during that time) should move into the room. It helps to have multiples of these staffs (they are not no-zone) because it increases your chances of destroying the pillar fast. Keep collecting them for your next tries if you cannot kill him first time.

Start using the staffs on the pillars until the pillars are destroyed, which can take some time. You will get a zone-wide message for that again. When the pillars are destroyed, finish off named and you are done with the zone.

Mob will cast Touch of the Faceless on MT every 40s, which will do between 40K and 80K damage. If needed, have OT's Intercede to absorb Touch of the Faceless. Also has a Mana Burn that will instantly drain power of your party.

Comes grouped with the following as an adventure party that your raid must beat:
- [Blorgok the Brutal](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Blorgok_the_Brutal) - Fighter
- [Meldrath Kloktik](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Meldrath_Kloktik) - Mage
- 2 [Froglok Mercenaries](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_froglok_mercenary) - Fighters
- [Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov) - Scout

This is a group encounter. The participants are:

**Jracol Binari** - Healer 
- ~1.3 million HP
- Will periodically group heal for ~200K

**Blorgok the Brutal** - Fighter 
- ~1 million HP    
- Will periodically force you to target him, at which point you are unable to change targets

**Meldrath Kloktik** - Mage 
- ~2.7 million HP
- Periodic stifle, knockback on melee hit (is a buff, dispel if you can)

**2 Froglok Mercenaries** - Fighters 
- ~200K HP each
- Will taunt and force you to switch targets, but you are able to change back to your previous target

**Ludmila Kystov** - Scout 
- ~4.9 million HP
- Will periodically change targets and go after someone else in the raid, charms males on occasion

If you did not successfully kill Doomcoil, Meldrath will summon him to aid in this fight. To avoid this, you must kill Meldrath before any of the other named NPCs. 

If you did kill Doomcoil, the recommended order is Jracol, Blorgok, Meldrath, and then Ludmila. The froglok mercenaries can be taken out at any point, or taken out by AoEs. DPS should target Jracol directly as the MT (and others) will be taunted to Blorgok and unable to change back. When other members of the raid are targeted by Ludmila they should run to the MT to make it easier for the tank to recover aggro. 

Our usual way on this group is to offtank Blorgok a bit away from raid to avoid people getting taunted too much, then full dps first on frogs (they die fast) and then healer (has to die fast too or else his heals will make fight harder). The main tank has a lot to do keeping aggro on Ludmila, so if you get troubles with aggro you can use another tank to keep aggro on the mob you're killing right now. If you have aggro problems then tell people to hold back with aoes.
Overall, its a rather easy fight, it's just about keeping aggro and healing the tanks. But if your main tank keeps losing aggro on Ludmila you will have a hard time beating it.

Comes grouped with the following as an adventure party that your raid must beat:
- [Jracol Binari](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Jracol_Binari) - Healer
- [Blorgok the Brutal](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Blorgok_the_Brutal) - Fighter
- 2 [Froglok Mercenaries](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_froglok_mercenary) - Fighters
- [Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov) - Scout

Healers only heal and cure and don't do damage of any kind.

1-2 people devoted to clicking the statues in his room. Statues must be clicked constantly during the fight to prevent the two larger statues in the hallway leading up to his room from spawning.

During the fight Venril will cast Toxic Infusion, which is an AOE that is on a 38s timer. You have 6 seconds to cure this Noxious effect from your raid. Cure it or die from a follow-up spell called Fatalisis. Bring potions in case you don't have group noxious cures in every group.

At 65% Venril will emote how he will finish the fight. Use the [Mistmyrian Soulcube](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Mistmyrian_Soulcube) from [Tairiza the Widow Mistress](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tairiza_the_Widow_Mistress) within 5 seconds or die. 

At 60%, with Venril's main ability disabled, he will begin to watch your power consumption. Your raid force must keep their power around 30% or higher (the 70% power ceiling that used to be on the encounter is no longer in place) to be safe. A raid member who get's under 30% for too long will spawn adds that will wipe the raid most likely. 

On top of just maintaining your power, Venril will also place 2 kinds of effects on 2 random members every 45 seconds. These effects last for about 10-12 seconds and make it more challenging to keep your power in the target zone.

Mana Sacrifice causes you to consume 200% more power than usual. Do not use any abilities that use power while this effect is on you. If you can, turn up your particle effects because you will glow 'BLUE' when Mana Sacrifice is on you. If you are in the middle of casting anything and the effect lands on you cancel it immediately.

The other ability is the opposite. It gives the target enhanced power regen. A feed of about 1% power every 2 seconds for 10-12 seconds.

**If you are well geared 90+ you can easily solo it using "tank&spank" strategy, though you will still need the Mistmyrian Soulcube. If you kill Sathir without the Soulcube, your entire group/raid will be hit for 100,000+ focus damage and Sathir will reset rather than dying.**

Position MT in one corner nearest the door to his room.  Raid in other corner near door. MT pulls, OT pulls adds to corner opposite MT.  Healers stay in corner near door, away from MT.  All casters go to opposite corner and stay there.  Burn adds, then name.  Melee jousts Spinning Fury (50s timer). Mob is quite easy.

Disease based AoE Fear & DoT called Judgement of the Faceless (every 45s) does more damage the closer you are. MT pulls to far left corner with Sanctuary up. Everyone else stays outside of the room until Judgement of the Faceless hits, then rush in. Joust all the way out of the room every 45s before Judgement hits then back in after. Adds can be mezzed, or burn them down first.

zones.background_md: A group of renegade [Sabertooth Gnolls](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Sabertooth_Gnolls_(Faction)) are threatening to attack! You must get in there and stop them!
zones.notes_md:
- Guild Raid Zone: Enter from door near the guild registrar in [Freeport](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_City_of_Freeport) or [Qeynos](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Qeynos_Capitol_District) in [The Coldwind Crier](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Coldwind_Crier) at (564.71, -9.79, 163.53).
- Recommended that all raiders, Good or Evil, be in either Freeport or Qeynos Capitol District when zone is entered.
- If you do have both Good and Evil characters doing this quest for the guild, make sure that players first pick up the quest from their respective cities.

other_sections.objectives:
**Kill [High Shaman Vascha](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/High_Shaman_Vascha)**:
- After killing all the trash, take down the gnolls in the druid ring. Then kill the large encounter of gnolls inside the ring. After that, the High Priest will summon an elemental to attack you (random; earth, fire, water or air).
- Immediately after killing the elemental the High Priest becomes attackable. He doesn't have any special tactics, but will spawn about 2-3 Heroic mobs every twenty seconds or so.
  - A good strategy is to have everyone focus on taking down the main boss as fast as possible, with the tanks sending out AoEs to bring down the adds. It is best to clump together so no one is sitting with an add out of range of the tank's AoEs.
- [High Shaman Vascha](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/High_Shaman_Vascha) is a healer, most likely a defiler. He is mostly (~75%) crushing melee, while using a couple disease spells (damage and debuff).
  - Doesn't use wards, only direct heals.

zones.background_md:
The Temple of Kor-sha is the sacred district of [Chardok](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Chardok). No one is allowed inside save the most trusted of [Overking Selrach Di'Zok](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Selrach_Di'Zok), ruler of the [pureblood Sarnak](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Di'Zok). For the past months, the Overking has not left the confines of the temple, conducting his research in strict privacy. It is written that the [sarnak](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Sarnak) cradle of creation was the [mythical workshop of King Atrebe](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Emperor_Atrebe's_Laboratory:_The_Fabled_City_of_Kor-sha), the creator. The Overking created the Temple workshop in Chardok in honor of this workshop of [Atrebe](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Atrebe_Sathir), but it is rumored that he is working on awakening a project of old, Project Gorowyn.

Project Gorowyn was an ancient attempt to reproduce King Artebe's work so that the sarnak may create a savior race that no empire could stand up to. The project never came to be, but the sacred texts that sired it now rest in the Temple of Kor-sha with the Overking. Through his research, Selrach has been able to create his version of this savior race, the Immortals. They appear in likeness to the sarnak but are larger, stronger, and never tire. Fortuantley, the Overking has yet to produce them in large enough numbers to pose a substantial threat to the other denizens of [Kunark](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Kunark), so they are mainly being used to guard [Queen Velazul Dizok](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Queen_Velazul_Dizok) and the Overking himself. *(Source: [Warlord Gadar Gwalnax](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Warlord_Gadar_Gwalnax))*

Although the original Kor-sha was never discovered by the sarnak, a number of the artifacts from this place have made their way into the Kunarkian black markets. Over decades, the Overking has acquired many of these artifacts and stored them within the Temple. Since they come from the birthplace of the sarnak, Selrach hopes they hold the key to furthering the genetic evolution of the sarnak, and eventually convert himself into an Immortal.
zones.strategy_md:
This is a tier 2 Kunark raiding zone.

Once inside the Temple you will need to watch out for wandering 83x4 mobs. There are 5 or 6 of these wandering mobs which come in pairs roaming throughout the zone, and they roam a LONG way. One pair of these mobs will come almost all the way to the zone in point.  They come in pairs (a Charnak soldier and a Chokidai tracker) but may be far apart -- have the MT taunt the soldier and the OT taunt the tracker.

From the zone in point your raid force will need to head either left or right up the stairs. No wandering mobs up there, just make sure your raid force avoids pulling the first wandering mob as you proceed up the stairs. Kill the 83x4 a runic guardian (non-aggro simple tank and spank) in both the room to the right and the room to the left. Go back to the entrance and proceed to kill the first wandering pair then proceed into the first lower room to kill another Runic Guardian. 

Proceed into the next hallway, continuing to keep an eye out for wandering pairs. The next room is a non-attackable Atrebe. Kill off a couple of the other wandering pairs of Soilder/dog combos. Enter into the next hallway making your first available left down yet another hallway. Follow this hallway around to a circular room and kill the Runic Guardian that stands there. Proceed down the stairs all the way and kill more Runic Guardians in the room to the left and right. 

After you have killed these two runic guardians send the entire group up to the first flight of stairs. Make sure the tank has enough healing to keep him alive while is pulls the named (Uzdrak the Invincible). The rest of the raid force will stay at the bottom of the stairs with the OT and prepare to pull Ilyan and Entyr.  Entyr will cast an Arcane effect that needs to be cured immediatly and Ilyan will cast some type of Elemental.

One strategy is to send the entire raid force to fight Uzdrak and debuffing him thoroughly.  Make sure the MT is parked in a corner and the rest of the raid is parked in the opposite corner.  Watch the AoE's and KB's.  At 80%, the MT group stays on Uzdrak while the rest of the raid goes down the stairs and to the right to pull Entyr.  Entyr will cast an arcane effect that needs to be cured immediately.  Once Entyr is dead, the raid runs back up to pound on Uzdrak some more with lots of debuffs.  At 50%, the raid runs down to Ilyan.  Ilyan will cast an elemental.  Once Ilyan is down, go back up to the MT group to finish off Uzdrak.

Everyone proceed back up the stairs and make a left. In this room there are 5 aggro mobs 2 of which are named and also another Runic Guardian.  Pull the Runic Guardian into the hallway to finish him off.  One strat is to split the nameds, another is just have your MT tank em all.  Pull them into the hallway to keep the fight contained.  Use the timers on the AoE and use AoE blockers.  Buff for poison and disease resists.  Order of the kills is excavationers, Uthtak the Cruel, and Aktar the Dark.  After the fight, in the room they were standing is a small box that your Monk, Bruiser or SK should collect for the next fight. 

Everyone now moves back to the unattackable Atrebe's room. MT park in the corner everyone else but their backs against one of the pillars or on the stairs. The person who collected the box can spawn Atrebe.  Atrebe has a knock forward that will fling the raid. Get the AoE timers use AoE blockers and easy enough.

Continue the way you were going until you reach the atrium -- it will have 4 waterfalls in the corners.  Have the raid stay in the hallway while the MT pulls the roamer back to the raid for an easy kill.  There are two assassins hidden in the waterfalls.  Have the MT step into the water to spawn them and pull them back to the raid for some more easy kills.

There are two smaller hallways to the north and east.  Each end in a room that contains a named, Zarda and Kodux.  Pull the extra mob in each room and kill them in the hallway before beginning the next fight.

The two named need to be fought together, but there is a trick.  They need to be kept as far apart as possible or else they will cast an AOE that will wipe the raid.  Their health also needs to be within 30% of each other.  During the fight, they will target random people in the raid and port them into the center.  

One strategy is to divide the raid into two groups and stand in the north and east hallways to fight the twins.  Everyone targets through their mob.  When the twins teleport someone, have one person from each group call out the name of the person teleported and the direction they should run.  This helps to bring the mobs back to the tanks quickly and prevent the twins from meeting up.  Tanks should stand in the hallway but near the atrium to taunt the mobs after each teleport.  Give yourself a well-deserved pat on the back for that fight. Stupidly insane ability to teleport people into the atrium so both twins go after them makes this a pointless fight.  Just ignore them, they aren't needed to kill the boss.

Head down the west hallway, killing another set of roamers and two more Runic Guardians.

other_sections.soloing quickguide: The instance is straight forward. The only non-intrinsic part is activating [Atrebe's Statue](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Atrebe's_Statue). To activate this boss, defeat [Aktar the Dark](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Aktar_the_Dark) and [Uthtak the Cruel](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Uthtak_the_Cruel), loot *a strange box* at (1071,-48,367) and then right click Atrebe's Statue to start the encounter.

encounters.strategy_md:
This mob is a non-loot mob. The only thing you get from this fight is the device to activate Atrebe. It is best to duel tank this mob due to a noxious stun that is also a nasty uncurable dot. The spell is Noxious Mist and has a seemingly 50 sec. recast to it. It is best to joust this AoE to avoid it. 

The offtank needs to stick Uthtak the Cruel with an arrow and pull his and the adds to the corner oppisite where the raid force is. The adds will taunt the offtank and force him to stay on that target, best to stay on that target and burn it. The MT needs to taunt Aktar the Dark away from the raid force to avoid the AoE and tank him about where he stands. Again, the AoE is nasty and uncurable as well as stuns. Uthtak has a reflect shield on him so be careful with your big nukes and big debuffs...they can be reflected back to you and your group. Once Uthtak is down have the raid force joust Aktar's AoE and burn him. 

Again, don't be alarmed when a box does not hit the ground after the encounter is over.

One person needs to loot and equip [a cryptic device](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_cryptic_device) dropped from the Uthtak/Aktar fight.  They can then right-click on Atrebe's Statue, and select "Press Glowing Gem on the Cryptic Device" to make Atrebe's Statue attackable.

Maximize Poison & Magic resists

Joust the two AoEs, which increase in damage the closer you are to the mob. Cure immediately with healers or potions when hit with either one. 

**Sebilisian Sporecloud**
- At 0 to 10 meters, 12338-24492 poison damage plus 2903-5763 poison damage every 5 seconds
- At 10 to 15 meters, 7583-11374 poison damage plus 2166-3250 poison damage every 5 seconds
- At 15 to 75 meters, 4875-5958 poison damage plus 1462-1787 poison damage every 5 seconds
- For ACT the spell needs to read "Statue's Sebilisian Sporecloud" and goes off at about the 45 sec. mark

**Force of Atrebe**
- You will be launched into the air and hurled toward Atrebe's Statue
- At 0 to 10 meters, 7936-23808 magic damage plus 3174-9523 magic damage every 9 seconds
- At 10 to 15 meters, 5079-11851 magic damage plus 1905-4444 magic damage every 9 seconds
- At 15 to 100 meters, 3809-5714 magic damage plus 1693-2540 magic damage every 9 seconds
- For ACT the spell needs to read "Statue's Force of Atrebe" and goes off at about the 40 sec. mark

Force of atrebe actually kicks you in the direction you are facing, so if you face mob it will pull you to him if you face away you will get a standard kb. 
Resists, fast cures for the dots, jousting and a bit luck (if both aoes go off at same time or very close it is very likely that it kills MT) are the keys to this fight. if healers cannot cure fast enough then bring potions.
- For the house item, see [Rise of Kunark (House Item)](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Rise_of_Kunark_(House_Item)).

- Control-immune.
- Has a knockback - good spot to fight her is behind her throne, right at the wall.
- Has a charm so it makes it hard to solo.

The Overking is the final mob in the Temple of Korsha. Make sure the assassins are cleared out of the pools before engaging him or they will join the fight. Use elemental resist gear if you are low, and have the Troubador run Elemental Chorus. The off-tank group should be a good AoE setup. For example a Paladin with amends on a warlock, a Shadowknight or Berserker.

The main tank group can use a third healer to deal with the incredible amount of curing required. Ideal setup for your first time would be Guardian, Templar, Defiler, Warden, Coercer, Dirge. Keeping this group cured is important due to the large amount of stun/stifles caused by the Mentalist adds. Having your healers set up /tell macros to assigned casters to cure them is a good idea also.

Due to the fact the main tank will be frequently taunted off the Overking, it is a good idea to make a target macro that you can keep clicking to get your target back: /target Selrach

Everyone should setup just before the two pillars. The fight will start with the tank running to the throne and holding the Overking there. The rest of the raid goes into a tight group directly in the middle of the two pillars and starts casting AoEs. The off-tank should pull the adds to the raid and hold them there so AOEs have maximum effect, keeping them away from the main tank.

Once the first set of adds are dead you will get a continuous spawn of weaker adds that have stunning casters called Mentalists. When these waves spawn, kill down the Mentalists first. Once you have the waves under control, you can assign more single target damage to attack the Overking with the main tank. Figuring out just the right number of people you can take off the adds and put on the Overking will speed this fight up.

During the fight two AoEs will go off. One is called Concusive Blast. It is magic based, hits every 52 seconds, and is fairly weak. The AoE of concern is the heat based one that hits every 30 seconds. This is the reason the Overking must be kept away from the raid and using a tank with good heat resists like a Guardian helps on the healing.

Once things are under control and the Overking is at about 80% health, the tank should move the Overking from the throne past the pillars. Wait for the mentalists to be dead from the current set of adds, and for the heat AoE to have gone off. Once the heat DoT has been cured and there are only a couple adds up, pull the Overking through the raid force and onto the other side. Thought snap is useful here.

The tank should be far enough from the raid that the heat AoE doesn't do much damage to them. Don't pull against the wall though, you need to be in the center of the left area so when the flame adds come they don't all come around the side.

Once you get him to 58% he is going to start speaking. At this point make sure the person who has the [Wand of Intervention](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Isydryl's_Wand_of_Intervention) from [Pawbuster](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Pawbuster) is cured of all effects so they can use the wand on the Overking. This will disable his brutal AoE. The tank should use any damage prevention (such as Tower of Stone) at this point, because the Overking will cast an additional AoE.

The Overking is now upset and will begin calling in the flames from the fire pots around his throne to come help him. If they reach the Overking he gets to do his AoE again which will likely kill everyone. If you target the flames directly without them getting far enough away from the throne area, they will port you to the Overking and he will kill you. The trick to this is to let them funnel past the pillars to the raid force and use blue AoEs to destroy the flames without directly targeting them.

There will be a couple of flames that sneak around the pillars on the outer sides. Call these and have a brig or some other class assigned to go and kill them. Have a few AoE classes continue to kill the flames, and the rest of the raid can focus DPS on the Overking.
See [Aktar the Dark](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Aktar_the_Dark) for more information.

zones.background_md: The most fearsome arsenal of dragonkind dwell here, protecting the [inner sanctum](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Temple_of_Scale:_Pedestal_of_the_Priestess). This is the temple of the Awakened, and the uninitiated will be torn limb from limb.

other_sections.geography: This is a contested raid, meaning multiple, unaffiliated raid groups can enter here, potentially leading to disputes over boss encounters. The layout is identical to [Sanctum of the Scaleborn](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sanctum_of_the_Scaleborn).

metadata.iname2: Temple of scale pedestal of priestess entrance.jpg

zones.background_md: High Priestess of the Awakened, [Harla Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Harla_Dar), dwells here, spreading her thoughts and emotions to all dragon-folk within the Overrealm. [Ireatan the Scale Eye](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ireatan_the_Scale_Eye) explains that when the High Priestess dreams, her great power forces those around her to dream the same dream. Perpetually in mourning of her murdered husband and son, Harla Dar will not be too welcoming of those who would disturb her grief.

encounters.strategy_md: Clear all drakes on the lower platform in a (clockwise or counterclockwise), leaving the droags for a second clearing, as these have a shorter respawn time. KILL FAST, aiming for clearing in 10-15 minutes! If you're not averaging 13k DPS or better don't bother do this. Move raid to top of stairs and body pull the drakes, again in a clockwise or counterclockwise manner. All can be pulled without aggro'ing Harla Dar. Once all drakes and droags are clear, pull Harla Dar to the blocked wall behind the area where she stands (the zone out) and be careful of knockback.

zones.background_md:
[Thuuga](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Thuuga_the_Serpent_Lord) was the chieftain of a small clan that can be traced back to the [Iksar Kylong Tribe](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Iksar_Lore). It was during the [The Age of Enlightenment](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Age_of_Enlightenment) that Thuuga's tribe existed in a small city in [the Dreadlands](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/the_Dreadlands). That city was called Drolakis. Thuuga's power of sorcery and his powerful tribe of warriors were subjugated by the resurrected [Venril Sathir](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Venril_Sathir). Along with their subjugation came the curse that turned the people of Drolakis into an army of [Drolvarg](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Drolvarg). These beasts would become Venril's army in the south.

Thuuga's love of his homeland had him create a secret throne that was based on the architecture of the city of Drolakis. When the chieftain was slain in battle, his life force was spirited away to that hidden dwelling. That resting place was the only intact remainder of a secret throne of the drolvarg commander. His secrets of Sathirian betrayal and Chelsith history have been entombed here until recently when the [Drachnid](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Drachnid) discovered it.
zones.strategy_md:
It might be smart to have two solid tanks for this raid. It's advisable that you have a Crusader with the [Fearless Morale](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Fearless_Morale) AA ability. Success in this zone depends greatly on the raid's ability to withstand three things: fear, stun, and the cocoons (crushing). 

Clearing the entire zone of trash mobs (drachnids and skeletons) is the first goal.  All skeletons will fear, so crouching and walking while fighting the skeletons will minimize the impact of this when it happens. Try to make these every 2nd pull, so the MT templar can have [Sanctuary](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sanctuary) up. 

Once all trash is cleared, you can pull [Tairiza the Widow Mistress](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tairiza_the_Widow_Mistress). Pull her to the small room just outside her larger room, the small space allows the MT to pull her to the left and the raid can perch on the right side everyone within AOE range of each other. Have a MA/OT stand in the center of the hallway between the raid and the MT just behind The Widow Mistress and have the raid and all the DPS target through the MA. 

Tairiza will cocoon PCs randomly and knockback. For this reason pull her out of her room into that smaller space (such as the corridor) During the fight she will randomly "cocoon" players, when cocooned the player will not be able to cast, attack or move.  However, mana stone and hearts and shards still work. The cocoons have to be destroyed to free the Player.

All DPS on the raid should target through MA; having the MA crate a macro (such as these two lines: */target a digestive sac* and */target Tairiza the Widow Mistress*), the MA can spam the macro during the fight focusing the raid's DPS on the sacs as they form and the retarget Tairiza as they are cleared. AoE damage here will continue to to help kill the widow mistress, the cocoons and any spawned adds all at the same time. Multiple cocoons can be up if you start falling behind and if to many people get sacked or cocooned you will lose a lot of DPS.

  - Before pulling Tairiza, have everyone in the raid make a macro saying, "/r *NAME* IS SACKED" 
  - Digestive sacs have approx 75,000 health and will hit for 750 crushing every second with a stun.

At a certain health percentage (~40%), Tairiza spawns several heroic adds. This can eventually become overwhelming if they are not killed quickly, so it is advised you have an off-tank with high AoE threat and enough players with high AoE damage.

You can either continue to kill the cocoons, or depending on your raid DPS, ignore them and burn at this stage.
zones.notes_md:
- Try to conserve as much power throughout the fight with Tairiza as possible. Recommend shards/hearts are given and manastones are used, as well as having several power regen classes. 
- Tairiza will cast Bolster Confidence which heals her for about 135,000 every 30 seconds. She also has a stun that hits about every 25 seconds or so called Fearsome Gaze, and a disease lifetap named Necrosis. If you are using Advanced Combat Tracker (ACT) you can create timers for these spells.
- Tairiza has approximately 5,500,000 HP.
- Tairiza heals herself on the death of any player character.

encounters.strategy_md:
This is really a burn fight.
Tairiza spawn "sacs" approximately every 30 seconds.  These sac will land on a random person in the raid which will incapacitate them as well as kill them over time.
Have ranged DPS and healers to the left or right of the door with a Ranged DPS main assist calling the sacs and making the ranged dps cycle between killing the sacs then immediately back on the named.
Melee DPS needs to stand right under the big butt of the Widow Mistress and target the mob directly and BURN IT DOWN.  If a sac spawns on melee dps or the Main Tank it's easy for the Melee dps to just TAB, burn the sac down, then resume beating the spider.
At approximately 40% she spawns a group of spiders.  Once this happens pretty much ignore the sacs and focus the burn on the mob.  A zerker or strong AE'ing offtank needs to try to get as many spiders beating on them as possible.  

NOTE: The spider adds are immune to all taunts. Another option if you have multiple Enchanters is to mezz them. We still do it that way. Either way you want to burn Tairiza as fast as possible once the adds start. If you have big spells/CAs with long recast timers, make sure you save them for that final push.

Once the spiders pop everyone in the raid target the named directly and just BURN BURN BURN.  Some people will die.  They need to revive and get back in.  AE's will burn a lot of the spiders down, but prolly not all of them so once you kill the named kill the remaining spiders and loot the metal chest.

Healers only heal and cure and don't do damage of any kind.

1-2 people devoted to clicking the statues in his room. Statues must be clicked constantly during the fight to prevent the two larger statues in the hallway leading up to his room from spawning.

During the fight Venril will cast Toxic Infusion, which is an AOE that is on a 38s timer. You have 6 seconds to cure this Noxious effect from your raid. Cure it or die from a follow-up spell called Fatalisis. Bring potions in case you don't have group noxious cures in every group.

At 65% Venril will emote how he will finish the fight. Use the [Mistmyrian Soulcube](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Mistmyrian_Soulcube) from [Tairiza the Widow Mistress](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tairiza_the_Widow_Mistress) within 5 seconds or die. 

At 60%, with Venril's main ability disabled, he will begin to watch your power consumption. Your raid force must keep their power around 30% or higher (the 70% power ceiling that used to be on the encounter is no longer in place) to be safe. A raid member who get's under 30% for too long will spawn adds that will wipe the raid most likely. 

On top of just maintaining your power, Venril will also place 2 kinds of effects on 2 random members every 45 seconds. These effects last for about 10-12 seconds and make it more challenging to keep your power in the target zone.

Mana Sacrifice causes you to consume 200% more power than usual. Do not use any abilities that use power while this effect is on you. If you can, turn up your particle effects because you will glow 'BLUE' when Mana Sacrifice is on you. If you are in the middle of casting anything and the effect lands on you cancel it immediately.

The other ability is the opposite. It gives the target enhanced power regen. A feed of about 1% power every 2 seconds for 10-12 seconds.

**If you are well geared 90+ you can easily solo it using "tank&spank" strategy, though you will still need the Mistmyrian Soulcube. If you kill Sathir without the Soulcube, your entire group/raid will be hit for 100,000+ focus damage and Sathir will reset rather than dying.**

zones.background_md: You've stumbled upon the entrance to a hidden lagoon. A peek inside reveals [lizardmen](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Lizardman) involved in some bizarre ritual. Instantly, dread snuffs out any courage you may have had coming into this place. Do you retreat in fear or explore what more [the Feerrott](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/the_Feerrott) has to hide?
zones.walkthrough_md:
1. Begin killing every lizardman in the jungle/beachhead.
1. Once most are dead, kill the [Alliz Tae Soultaker](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Alliz_Tae_Soultaker) at (-996, -8, 211) and [Asogwe Greths remains](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Asogwe_Greths_remains), patrolling around that same location.
1. Kill the [Alliz Tae Spiritcaller](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Alliz_Tae_Spiritcaller) at (-830, -7, 214) and [Soonogs remains](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Soonogs_remains), patrolling around that same location.
1. Swim over to the isle at the center of the lagoon and slay all the [protectors of fear](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/A_protector_of_fear).
1. Hail the Alliz Tae summoner NPC, It will ignore you but two in-game messages will appear in your chat box indicating that [Alizasaur](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Alizasaur) is spawning.
1. Slay [Alizasaur](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Alizasaur), who will spawn underwater at (-974, -17, -16), make his way to the summoner NPC, kill him, and then return to his spawn location.

zones.background_md: A group of [Unkempt Druids](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Unkempt_Druids) have assembled in the [Shattered Vale](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Shattered_Vale). They are seeking a temporary alliance between wild entities of the natural realm in an effort to attack [Qeynos](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Qeynos) and [Freeport](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_City_of_Freeport). This cannot be allowed to transpire!

other_sections.objectives:
**Eliminate the conspirators**:
- Take down the four conspirators in the zone: three ambassadors to aspects of nature (druids, nymphs and pixies) and finally the main giant tree.
- There are wandering mobs all around, just like in [The Shattered Vale: A Bold Confrontation](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Shattered_Vale:_A_Bold_Confrontation). Heroic mobs are interspersed with epics throughout the entire area.
  - You'll also notice a ring of treants surrounding the Druid Ring as well, however they are non-aggro. It is recommnded to clear them out anyway, just in case they become active during a boss fight.
- The first ambassador is to the left in near the tents, another is to the right in a little bend, and another is back behind the druid ring.
  - The first ambassador (druid) is to the left in the tents and will drop a chest. He has a minor stun, but that's about it.
  - The second ambassador (nymph) is to the right and doesn't drop anything. She has a brief charm.
  - The third ambassador (pixies) is in the back.

The most elite of the [Renda'Dal](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Renda'Dal) can be found here, guarding their queen with great fervor.
