zones.background_md: [Trakanon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trakanon) is a general of the [Ring of Scale](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Ring_of_Scale_(Faction)). After the death of [Jaled Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Jaled_Dar_(NPC)) at the hands of [Sebilisian Emperor Ganak](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Dominus_Ganak), Trakanon, with dragon allies, descended on what was left of [Sebilis](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilis) and burnt it to the ground. He now bides his time at the summit of [Veeshan's Peak](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Veeshan's_Peak_(POI)), plotting his next move.
zones.notes_md: - Added in [LU51](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Update:51), players who have completed the quest, [Taking on Trakanon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taking_on_Trakanon), can speak with [Snyr'dok](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Snyr'dok) in [Veeshan's Peak](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Veeshan's_Peak) and teleport directly to the entrance teleporter.

encounters.strategy_md: - If you have finished the quest, [Taking on Trakanon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taking_on_Trakanon), he will offer to teleport you to the entrance of [Trakanon's Lair](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trakanon's_Lair).

I recently duo'd Trakanon with a guildie's Mystic and our two mercs.  We didn't have a Chelsith stone or anything, but still managed to kill him.  It was a tough fight though.  He's definitely much more doable now in COE end game gear.  Gonna' get me one of those cool looking dragon head shields sooner or later!

- When you enter you are in a zone in/zone out room which leads to gigantic stairs. You must use the side area of the stairs to walk up into the main lair.

- When you first enter the zone, trakanon will greet you from far above the platform, then fly down. At this point you can engage trakanon.

- You must have a chelsith stone with you to have any chance to succeed. It will drop in the leviathan encounter in-game. (Obsolete as of end-game CoE, heroic ToV, If you have a tank with you, otherwise you will probably still need an orb.)

- Once the fight begins, you will notice the holder of a chelsith stone can touch the orb altar on the far side of the platform to energize it. Once energized if you stay within 14m of the energized orb structure, you are protected from many of trakanons more powerful abilities.

- The key to winning this encounter is to realize you must all fight within 14m of the activated orb on the far side of the platform. Trakanons arcane maelstrom breath weapon is far too lethal outside this protective area. In addition trakanons knockback abilities will prove problematic, and all but one of them is negated when you are close to this orb object (if its energized)

- Trakanon is a level 87 warlock. He will periodically cast Wing Buffet and Tail Swipe. Wing buffet will STUN everyone in front of him for 2.5 seconds. It will also KNOCK everyone back UNLESS they are within 14m of the energized orb. Tail swipe does some damage, and stuns players for about 3 seconds. It will also KNOCK BACK any players that are NOT within 14m of the energized orb.

- Trakanon is immune to all control spells, and will double attack on melee 10% more than normal.

- Trakanon has Arcane Aura of the Wyrmlord on him. On take damage 10% of the time, players will be hit with Pestilential Pause. This does a slight amount of damage to the player and for the next 10 seconds, all the players spells take much longer to cast, and are basically at half effectiveness (heals and damage are half as effective as normal). Also your attack speed is reduced considerably during this time. This effect is not dispellable. Also, 2% of the time trakanon is hit with a hostile spell, he will heal himself for 2%.

- Trakanon will keep PROPHETIC SCRUTINY on one player at all times. With this effect on you, any spells you cast are interrupted, if the spell is interruptable. Every time you start to cast a spell you will be hit with PROPHETIC BOLT, which a small amount of damage and interrupts the spell you are starting to cast. This should be active on one player in the raid at all times

- Trakanon will keep Prescient gaze on all players at all times. It should never be off you for more than a few seconds even if you die and are rezzed back in. It causes all players to mana burn, using health whenever they use mana. It also causes players to have prescient intervention cast on them 8% of the time they try and cast a beneficial spell. Prescient intervention interrupts the spell they are trying to cast, and stifles the player for 2 seconds, and does a small amount of damage to the players mana pool.

- One player will generally have MARK OF REPULSION on him/her. This spell will go off at some random time, never less than 30 secs after it was placed on you. It has an hourglass icon. When it goes off that player will be hit with static repulsion, a targeted AOE that WILL knock back all players within 22m of the person who had Mark of Repulsion on him/her. You are NEVER protected from this knockback, even if near the orb. You must move away from your raid party so as not to endanger them with your Mark of Repulsion/Static Repulsion combo.

- 3.5 seconds AFTER static repulsion goes off, Trakanon will cast Arcane Maelstrom. A powerful aoe that does heat damage, does damage to a random item in everyones inventory, and does massive MAGIC BASED health and mana damage UNLESS you are close to the energized orb. You avoid MAGIC BASED MANA AND HEALTH DAMAGE only if you are near the energized orb.

- Trakanon will periodically cast Fetid Cloud and Toxic Tempest as well. Fetid Cloud does disease based health damage the closer you are to trak the more disease damage you take, and slows your attack speed. If you have Toxic Tempest still on you when fetid cloud hits you, you will take ADDITIONAL MASSIVE poison based health damage.

- Toxic tempest works similarly to Fetid Cloud, toxic tempest does range relative, poison based health damage to all players. If you have fetid cloud on you when Toxic tempest hits you, you will take additional MASSIVE disease damage as well. Toxic tempest slows your movement rate as well.

- 800 seconds after the raid fight begins, the platform you fight on begins to lower into the lava. This takes about 20-30 secs, after that time you will be forced to fight trakanon in the lava, and it won't likely work well for you. If you hop away from the platform, you wont be protected by the orb so that's not really an option. If you stay near the orb, you will be covered in lava so that's not really an option. You have only about 820 secs to win this fight.

- If trakanon dies, the platform will rise. If trakanon resets, the platform will rise.

zones.background_md: Inside the largest active volcano of the [Skyfire Mountains](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Skyfire_Mountains), Veeshan's Peak is the nigh-impenetrable ancient fortress of the [Ring of Scale](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Ring_of_Scale_(Faction)), the dragons that rebelled against the [Claws of Veeshan](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Claws_of_Veeshan_(Faction)) and left with [Jaled Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Jaled_Dar's_Shade) to [Kunark](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Kunark_(Continent)). This is a fiery abode built by dragons, for dragons. None but the most steadfast of heroes may hope to leave this place unburnt!
zones.walkthrough_md:
Make sure to get the [Taking on Trakanon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taking_on_Trakanon) quest from [Snyr'dok](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Snyr'dok) so you can take out [Trakanon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trakanon) later. 

Start heading to the Abysmal Chasm. As soon as you enter, a firedrake sentry and 3-4 frenzied racnor will come after you and your raid. Once you clear them, clear the rest of the trash mobs in the area so your raid isn't attacked later during the raid. 

After that, go left from the where the drake attacked and move on to kill [Kluzen the Protector](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Kluzen_the_Protector). 

After you kill Kluzen, go through the tunnel and kill [Nexona](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Nexona).  Be careful not to go in the water; it will hurt you after a few seconds. 

Go back and go up the bridge and follow the path towards [Elder Ekron](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Elder_Ekron).  Be careful when going across the second bridge, as meteors will try to hit the raid and will deal moderate elemental damage to those affected. To the left there is a staircase going down to [Elder Ekron](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Elder_Ekron). 

After killing Ekron, go back up and through the tunnel and enter the Athenaeum of the Scale ( 1274, -370, 259 ) . Clear all the trash mobs before solving the puzzle to kill [Druushk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Druushk). Now have 2 people go to either side of the room, both flip the first lever. Now go kill [Druushk](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Druushk). 

After you've killed Druushk, go to his spawning room and teleport out. Go down the tunnel with draconian sentilels in it ( 23, -459, -342 ). Continue down until you reach [Taskmaster Nichok](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taskmaster_Nichok). If you want to be able to mend your gear, go to the left and talk with Nixvisss  ( 7, -450, 67 ) and he will ask you to kill the next few bosses. Now go to the right and collect a machine key by clicking on the bench at  (133, -459, -391 ) ; anyone in the raid can get on of these. You will need it for the [Silverwing](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Silverwing) fight later. 

Go to the left and go up the path ( 14, -459, -433 ). (Note: The path will take a left turn, DO NOT turn left, keep straight and go up the wall ( -24, -449, -444 ).  Then get everyone on the elevator and press the button. It is a box with an glowing orange symbol on it.  

Now go kill [Milyex Vioren](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Milyex_Vioren) ( 197, -204, -1027 ) . He has an enslaved engineer with him.  

Go to the left and through the tunnel at  165, -213, -708 ).  There are two bosses in that direction: [Qunard Ashenclaw](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Qunard_Ashenclaw) and [Hoshkar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Hoshkar).  When you enter the Clawed Vault ( 303, -148, -374 ) you will come across a Hive on the floor of the path. Examine it, you will be attacked by Cinder Wasps. Kill Cinder Wasps until  [Qunard Ashenclaw](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Qunard_Ashenclaw) pops. Then, continue through the tunnel until it opens up and you see [Hoshkar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Hoshkar) down below. Take the path to the left. There is a rock floating in the lava, when it comes by to you jump on it then to the platform Hoshkar is on. Then do the same on the way out. 

Go back to the fork  180, -213, -750 ) and head west (the other path) to [Xygoz](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Xygoz). Hail him trigger the fight. 

Use the teleporter to get back to the Chasm. Now head East through the passage ( -198, -451, 275 ). Continue until you come to the end. There will be two doors to the right guarded by Juggernaut Golems. Go through one of the doors. Travel south until you come to two more doors, take either one and follow the path to kill [Travenro the Skygazer](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Travenro_the_Skygazer).  

Turn South and continue to the portal. Step through the portal and kill [Silverwing](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Silverwing). Reprogram the 8 turrets starting at ( -805, -557, 788 ). There are 2 turrents up on the ledge. Continue through a tunnel ( -665, -553, 727 ) and past the teleporter to kill [Shade of Khalan Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Shade_of_Khalan_Dar) and [Phara Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Phara_Dar). 

Now go back to start, however you would like ( I used a totem of escape ) and talk to [Snyr'dok](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Snyr'dok) again. Do the turn in and he will offer to take you to [Trakanon's Lair](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trakanon's_Lair).  The portal to Trakanon's Lair that was once inactive, will now take you to him. Once there climb the stairs and kill [Trakanon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trakanon). Loot the trophy from his body.
zones.notes_md:
- [Trakanon's Lair](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trakanon's_Lair) is located deep within, but an [access quest](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taking_on_Trakanon) is required to enter.
- The Skyfire Mountain Post is the closest [sokokar post](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sokokar).

encounters.strategy_md: This is possibly the hardest hitting named in Veeshan's Peak. It is recommended that you have at least 3 good heals in the Main Tank group.  Healers should try to get Shield of Rainbow Hues to block Druushk's periodic Fear AE.

Have your Main Tank pull this mob toward the wall behind him, then have the MT group stay tight as LoS issues will come up from time to time. Rest of the raid will be assisting the Off Tank. At around 75% or so a message will pop up warning you that a mob will spawn, which is a heroic ^^^ add which has a pretty nasty AE (hits harder than the main boss). Have your OT group meet the add and be careful that they turn it away from the raid or else it means a wipe. At this time you will notice that a targetable but non-killable baby dragon will be running to groups of eggs. This mob will spawn three more adds (down arrows), but they still can wipe you fairly quickly. Once those are taken care of get back on named until next message about add incoming. Rinse and repeat.

Everybody except the tank stay on the little island that is circling round Hoshkar's "main" isle. 
You are protected there by an aura. The tank stays on the main isle kiting Hoshkar in range of the little moving island.

If Hoshkar goes in the lava he stoneskins. Pets are fine in this fight.

Kluzen is the easiest of the named monsters in [Veeshan's Peak](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Veeshan's_Peak). Before the fight, make sure to clear all of the nearby trash mobs, as they are roamers- don't forget the [Drakota](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Drakota) flying overhead.

Kluzen is practically a straight burn. He does a large AOE called wingbeat (45 seconds) that must be announced and jousted, but that is all.

After the engineer goes down for the first time, one decent healer can keep one decent MT alive vs. the Named.

MT pulls the named anywhere he wants.  Doesn't' much matter where, he doesn't do anything.

Raid assists the MA...on pull, MA should target a "generator" that has appeared somewhere around the circle that the named is flying around.  After the "generator" is down, take out the engineer.  After the engineer is down, he's easy to keep dead with a couple of melee dps'ers on him.  Keep him down.

"Generators" spawn ever 20ish seconds.  1 for the first 20% of the nameds health, then 2 for the next 20% of the nameds health and finally 3 spawn every 20ish seconds for the rest of the fight.  If any "generator" is still standing after 15 seconds of their appearance the Named will do a KB that will send everyone flying all over the place.  The named will also heal himself a good amount.

That's it...pretty simple fight as long as you have the dps to take out the generators when they start spawning 3 at a time.

DPS on Nexona must be kept greater than 10k at all times in order to avoid a nasty AE. An additional AoE, Noxious Rot, can be avoided if DPS is kept greater than 25k.

Most people I know pull Nexona down the ramp to the left corner. (Left upon entering the room)  MT group stays down there.  Assign 4 "shiny" collectors.  One will stand at each weapon rack on either side of the room on the main floor, one outside in the hallway, and one up top where Nexona is - three shinies spawn up there, in the middle back and BEHIND each of the two specimens.  Rest of the raid go up the ramp that is to the left when entering the room and stack on the "specimen".  MT cleric hammer pull or pet pull.  Once Nex is pulled entire raid force that is stacked on the "specimen" start kill the specimen. Do this as fast as you can, then move to the other side up top where another "specimen" is located and take it out fast.  Run down the ramp on the right side and take out the "specimen" there.  By this time one shiny should have been harvested. (Harvested shineys spawn an x2 add, and they do memwipes)  Whoever harvested that shiney needs to let the raid know where it (the x2 add) is coming from so they can kill it.  Leave the "specimen" that is in the middle of the lower section.  It's of little concern.  Move to where Nexona is being tanked and take out a 4th "specimen"  The 5th "specimen" in the middle will be consumed by Nex when she gets to 90% health, all it does is heal her to 100%.  Not worth wasting time on.  Now just focus on the mobs that spawn from when someone harvests a shiny, and dps on Nex in between.

When Nex gets to 45% two drake 83^^^heroics will spawn from the water that is in the lower section.  These are easily mezzed for the remainder of the fight.  Drakes should be mezzed whole fight or they will stun and knock back everyone trying to kill shiny adds.  Having a healer that can joust between the shiny group and the mezzers is very helpful in case one breaks.  Concentrate on the shiny spawned mobs and Nexona, and gratz on your Mythicals.

In the same group as [Shade of Khalan Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Shade_of_Khalan_Dar)

There are two methods of pulling and killing Phara Dar.  When you approach Phara Dar's room you will notice the [Shade of Khalan Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Shade_of_Khalan_Dar) in the center and a ramp leading up the left side which takes you to Phara Dar.  Upon pulling Phara Dar the Shade of Khalan Dar will immediately fly to wherever Phara Dar is located and assist in the battle.  It is imperative to have a balanced Main and Off Tank group for this fight as you will need to kill both of these named mobs at once.  

The first method is to send two groups (Main and an assisting group) up the ramp to Phara Dar and fight the Shade of Khalan Dar at the very bottom of the ramp with their back to the wall.  The drawback to this is that even with snaps and aggro lock the Shade of Khalan Dar will still fly up to Phara Dar 2/3 of the time and have to be dragged back down the ramp to the Off Tank and their assisting group.  This can result in a wipe of the MT group and a fight reset.  Once the OT group has killed the [Shade of Khalan Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Shade_of_Khalan_Dar) at the bottom of the ramp they should run up and assist in DPS on Phara Dar.

The second (easier method) is to pull Phara Dar and subsequently the Shade of Khalan Dar into the hallway that leads to the main room.  If you attempt this method the MT group and their assisting party should be located closest to the room entrance, the OT group and their assisting party should be located approximately 40 metres up the stairs.  Positioning Phara Dar on the bottom far right and the Shade of Khalan Dar on the upper far left corners of the staircase makes it easier for the OT and assisting group to switch over to Phara Dar once the shade is dead.

Please note:  The Shade of Khalan Dar will heal itself to full once during the fight, this is not a bug.

Minor KB, minor AoE if within 20 meters.  To spawn him, click the beehive.  ^^ orange heroics will come, about 4, two sets of them.  After they are done the Queen Bee will come.  She's a joke.  After she is dead Ashenclaw will spawn.

Again, minor KB and minor AoE, just drag him to one of the hallways and tank and spank him.

MT can pull this named out of the room, which is recommended.  Pull him up the ramp and have the MT park against one of the pillars in the upper hallway.  He has a pretty hefty AoE on a 1:35 second timer. (seen 55sec - 1min 45sec) Stand about 50 meters away and you should be good. (End of the ramp around the corner seems good.)

Silverwing may need to be pulled into the hallway because of a new healing-to-100% effect which was added in Gu53.

***Beginning when Silverwing's health hits 80%***, someone in the raid will be ultra-visioned. It will be announced zonewide: "(target)is now seeing through their mind's eye!"
That person will need to go deactivate the large warding statue by clicking the small device *on the floor next to the large statue*. It can be difficult to see on many computers, so be sure to hover over the floor all around the warding statues. Until that person deactivates the statue, Silverwing will be ***immune to damage***, but he ***will*** continue to AoE. 

- **Fighters** deactivate the first statue on the left when entering Silverwing's room. (SouthWest)
 
- **Scouts** deactivate the center far side opposite Silverwing. (East)

- **Mages** deactivate the statue opposite the fighters. (Northwest)  

- **Priests** deactivate the one where Silverwing resides before he is pulled. (West)

**Note:** 
If you have not reprogrammed the wards already you will ***not*** be able to deactivate them now.

***Starting at 60% of Silverwing's health,*** adds will start to spawn. A successful strategy is to mesmerize these until Silverwing is Immune, then focus on the adds while the ultra-visioned person works on deactivating their ward statue.  

Also, there are two levers flanking Silverwing's original position. Deactivating one will summon a droag to turn it back on. Put two people on those levers and you will have less problems with the stuns.

- If you have finished the quest, [Taking on Trakanon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taking_on_Trakanon), he will offer to teleport you to the entrance of [Trakanon's Lair](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trakanon's_Lair).

Entire raid starts by facing north, standing far north of the named near the rocks.  Have tank run up just close enough to bow shot the named then he needs to run back (get to at least 45 meters away). The entire raid must then run north until the everyone is ported to the little island the named is standing on.

After the raid gets ported to the island, they should continue running north about 30-45 meters away from the named (back by the rocks).  10 seconds after the port a massive AoE goes off.  This AoE will only hit people closer than that 30-45 meter range.  Once the AoE goes off the entire raid has about 15 seconds to run close enough to get some ranged dps on the mob.  After about 15 seconds of dps'ing everyone needs to start running north again until they are at least 45 meters away from the named. It may be helpful to cancel your current target so that you don't get turned around when ported, since you might end up running in the wrong direction.

If anyone is still within 45 meters of the named when he ports you, those people will get a snare (incurable -75% run speed). So this means that those people that got snared are not going to get far enough away when his massive AoE goes off. Sprint can be helpful if you are snared. If you die, do not wait to be resurrected, just revive and start heading north so that when he does his zonewide raid port, you will be brought back instantly and can start fighting again.

Continue this jousting until the mob is dead.  Adds spawn at 70%, 50% and 30%.
At about 50% the 2nd person in the first group will be enslaved (teleported away from the raid and unable to attack while doing a mining animation).

Gratz on your Mythicals.

- If trakanon dies, the platform will rise. If trakanon resets, the platform will rise.

This mob is a relatively simple tank and spank encounter.  

There is a small arcane AOE that can be easily avoided by standing at the edge of the room.  You will also find that there are floating balls of lightning.  These have a knockback and can cause your spells to interrupt.  The best method of avoiding these is to have the ranged DPS stand at the very edge of the room (you will notice that the texture of the floor is two different colors, the balls will not affect you if you stand on the lighter tile) with healers moving in to attend to tanks and melee DPS as needed.

Xygoz is quite a fun fight. The MT will start by clicking on him - if anyone else does, they will be attacked. During the fight he will fall asleep, twice- there are two different dreams that you must deal with. They can happen in any order, and happen at 80% and 40%. Anyone underneath Xygoz when he falls asleep will be instantly killed.

The first dream will spawn miniature versions of all the major dragons in the zone. They mock Xygoz for having no pants, so you must kill them. They are all heroic except for Trakanon, and not very hard.

The second dream will create a miniature doll of everyone in the raid. They spawn at the bottom of the ramp to the North (entrance) of the room. Everyone must pick up the doll of his/herself (double click), and the last person to do so will be the one that Xygoz attacks when he wakes up.  The MT does not necessarily have to pick up their doll, they can simply agro lock Xygoz when he awakens.  Leaving the MT's doll up for the remainder of the fight has no negative consequences for the raid.  Once you have double clicked your doll, jousting back to the rock will keep the area clear for those having a harder time finding the miniature version of their character. Note that this step occasionally bugs, and may reset the encounter- just keep trying.

Aside from the dreams and an incurable fear there are no special tactics to Xygoz, jousting to the rock will keep the raid from suffering the fear effect.

zones.background_md:
Within the bowels of [Sebilis](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Sebilis) rests the nigh impregnable Sathirian throne. From here, [Emperor Venril Sathir](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Venril_Sathir) rules over his mighty empire. History has taught Venril to trust no one. He allows few, if any, to access into his throne room, least of all non-[Sathirians](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Sathirian_(Faction)).

Guarding the throne room are constructs and wards taken from some of the greatest arcane societies of [Norrath](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Norrath). Venril has begun to feed upon a new faith to provide him protection from the entities that may have been blessed with the might necessary to overcome the defenses of his throne.
zones.strategy_md:
You must have killed [Tairiza the Widow Mistress](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tairiza_the_Widow_Mistress) and collected an item ( Mistmyrian Soulcube ) from her if you want to have any chance at killing VS. Give the item from Tairiza to someone that isn't urgently needed when fighting VS. 

Zone in to the main room.  The hall directly in front of you will spawn 2 84x4 mobs once you cross the threshold between the main room and the hall.  After these have been killed the position to which you pull Venril only matters to your preference.  However, be aware, that VS has a couple AoE that are ranged base.  If you can move more than 75 meters away from him before is AoE goes off you'll avoid it.  The closer to him, the more damage it will do.

The person who has looted the "item" off of Tazaria will spawn VS.  To do this all they have to do is run into the room that the non-aggro VS resides.  VS will not, or should not attack the person who ran into the room, instead he'll target someone else in the raid and head their direction.  The person with the "item" needs to run behind where VS was standing and prepare to click on the two small figurines that start to glow every 10 to 15 seconds.  If these two figurines are not disabled (right click, choose option), the statues standing in the hallway will spawn and kill you all.

Once VS is aggro the tank of course pulls him to a corner and Walk/Crawl.  (VS has a fear shield that fears anyone who hits him 60% of the time.  Curable)  VS is a fight that everyone needs to pay attention to.

From the start of the fight he has 3 aoes that he will cast.  The first one is shown to the right, Toxic infusion.  This is cast approximately every 32 seconds (although it varies and is very hard to time).  This needs to be cured asap.  VS will then cast Fatalis 6 seconds later.  If toxic infusion is still on the person when fatalis goes off it will explode their heart and ususally kill them instantly.  If the person happens to survive the fatalis, it will still drain all their power, and spawn adds if you are further into the fight. 

He also has a aoe stun that is cureable trauma.  This trauma is much like the one that Pawbuster casts.  Have your healers stand behind the dps, so that the DPS are hit with the trauma aoe.  Healers can then cure.

Healers do not want to attack durring this fight (just heal/cure/debuff) because of the fear DS.  If healer is feared durring toxic infusion it ususally means a wipe.

VS also has a spell reflect, it is minor it seems to be about 30% reflect..make sure your spell casters are aware.  it does not seem to reflect melee or CAs, but it does reflect debuffs/spells/songs.

When his health gets to 65-63% he will activate his ring. Everyone with a cube from Tairiza the Widow Mistress needs to start clicking it.  Have those people stop auto attack so they are not feared.  You must make sure the guardians are down at this point if they have spawned else they may force the person with the cube to change target. Once the cube goes off it deactivates his ring and your raid can stop clicking the cube (much like overkings sword).  If his ring is not deactivated, he will do massive consecutive aoes.

When VS get to 60% health he will start to watch your power, showing a green detriment uncurable icon in your detriment window and spawn adds for every person that has less than 40% power. 

He aslo casts a detrimental effect that looks like a purple icon in your detrimentals and it outlines your body in purple.  It is uncurable, and everytime you use a CA/Spell/Heal/Cure you will use 225% more power to cast it.  You do not want to cast anything while it is on you.  This detrimental also increases your power by 1-2% per tick for 13-15 seconds, and then it reduces your power by 10% when it ends.
zones.notes_md:
- Each player who dies will heal Venril ~2% of his maximum health.
- Use sprint to toggle your power, double clicking it will reduce your power by 15%, keeping it on and moving around will reduce it by 10% per tick after. Bards need to remove runspeed to use sprint.
- Sprint can be turned off while stunned but not feared, so turn off auto-attack while toggling sprint to avoid being feared while sprinting.
- If you are level 100+, you won't need to use the [Mistmyrian Soulcube](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Mistmyrian_Soulcube) because he can be killed in a single attack. You can use a chronomage to set your effective level to 80 for this zone.
- On Varsoon TLE, you will have to defeat Pawbuster, Tairiza and Sel'rach to enter Venril Sathir's lair. Or get zoned into by someone that has cleared them.

encounters.strategy_md:
This is really a burn fight.
Tairiza spawn "sacs" approximately every 30 seconds.  These sac will land on a random person in the raid which will incapacitate them as well as kill them over time.
Have ranged DPS and healers to the left or right of the door with a Ranged DPS main assist calling the sacs and making the ranged dps cycle between killing the sacs then immediately back on the named.
Melee DPS needs to stand right under the big butt of the Widow Mistress and target the mob directly and BURN IT DOWN.  If a sac spawns on melee dps or the Main Tank it's easy for the Melee dps to just TAB, burn the sac down, then resume beating the spider.
At approximately 40% she spawns a group of spiders.  Once this happens pretty much ignore the sacs and focus the burn on the mob.  A zerker or strong AE'ing offtank needs to try to get as many spiders beating on them as possible.  

NOTE: The spider adds are immune to all taunts. Another option if you have multiple Enchanters is to mezz them. We still do it that way. Either way you want to burn Tairiza as fast as possible once the adds start. If you have big spells/CAs with long recast timers, make sure you save them for that final push.

Once the spiders pop everyone in the raid target the named directly and just BURN BURN BURN.  Some people will die.  They need to revive and get back in.  AE's will burn a lot of the spiders down, but prolly not all of them so once you kill the named kill the remaining spiders and loot the metal chest.

Healers only heal and cure and don't do damage of any kind.

1-2 people devoted to clicking the statues in his room. Statues must be clicked constantly during the fight to prevent the two larger statues in the hallway leading up to his room from spawning.

During the fight Venril will cast Toxic Infusion, which is an AOE that is on a 38s timer. You have 6 seconds to cure this Noxious effect from your raid. Cure it or die from a follow-up spell called Fatalisis. Bring potions in case you don't have group noxious cures in every group.

At 65% Venril will emote how he will finish the fight. Use the [Mistmyrian Soulcube](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Mistmyrian_Soulcube) from [Tairiza the Widow Mistress](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Tairiza_the_Widow_Mistress) within 5 seconds or die. 

At 60%, with Venril's main ability disabled, he will begin to watch your power consumption. Your raid force must keep their power around 30% or higher (the 70% power ceiling that used to be on the encounter is no longer in place) to be safe. A raid member who get's under 30% for too long will spawn adds that will wipe the raid most likely. 

On top of just maintaining your power, Venril will also place 2 kinds of effects on 2 random members every 45 seconds. These effects last for about 10-12 seconds and make it more challenging to keep your power in the target zone.

Mana Sacrifice causes you to consume 200% more power than usual. Do not use any abilities that use power while this effect is on you. If you can, turn up your particle effects because you will glow 'BLUE' when Mana Sacrifice is on you. If you are in the middle of casting anything and the effect lands on you cancel it immediately.

The other ability is the opposite. It gives the target enhanced power regen. A feed of about 1% power every 2 seconds for 10-12 seconds.

**If you are well geared 90+ you can easily solo it using "tank&spank" strategy, though you will still need the Mistmyrian Soulcube. If you kill Sathir without the Soulcube, your entire group/raid will be hit for 100,000+ focus damage and Sathir will reset rather than dying.**
